rows.query: how do I book a flight and hotel for a business trip
rows.answer:
Here's how to book a flight and hotel for a business trip:

**Before Booking**
First, ensure you have a **Travel Profile** set up at https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/. Your profile must include a corporate or personal credit card (required to secure hotel reservations).

**Standard Bookings**
Log in to the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights and hotels.

**Last-Minute Bookings**
- **Within 6 hours of departure:** Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours.
- **Same-day travel outside the 6-hour window:** Book online through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool.

If you haven't set up your Travel Profile yet, refer to the Quick Start Guide relevant to your region on the booking tool site.

rows.query: can I fly business class and how much will the company pay back
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can fly business class, but Amazon will only reimburse the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class.**

You must pay the difference between the economy policy-approved fare and the business class upgrade **out of pocket**.

**How it works:**
- Book through the Self-Service Booking Tool (desktop) or contact a Travel Counselor
- You pay the full ticket cost upfront
- Submit only the policy-approved economy fare amount for reimbursement
- Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are **not reimbursable**

Your manager will receive an email notification about the upgrade, but no action is required from them.

For full details, refer to **section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: what happens if my booking comes up as out of policy
rows.answer:
## Out of Policy Bookings

An "Out of Policy" flag means your selection **exceeds policy guidelines**, but it **does not prevent you from completing your booking**.

### What triggers an Out of Policy flag?
- Airfare that isn't the Lowest Logical Fare in economy with a seat assignment
- Hotel rates above the lowest available
- Car rental rates above the preferred rate for the approved car type

### What you need to do:
When prompted, **provide a brief business reason** for your selection. This is used as an audit tool to balance flexibility with frugality.

### Valid examples of justified out-of-policy bookings:
- A hotel that's out of policy but closer to your business meetings
- An intermediate SUV during inclement weather for safety reasons

### Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation:
| | Can Complete Booking? |
|---|---|
| **Out of Policy** – exceeds guidelines but has a business reason | ✅ Yes, with a reason |
| **Policy Violation** – breaks a hard rule (e.g., booking first class) | ❌ No |

For full details, refer to the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#1).

rows.query: where do I find a trip I booked a few weeks ago
rows.answer:
Go to the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and select **Trips** (or **Trips Library**, depending on your region) from the main menu — this shows both current and past trips, including invoices.

If the trip is no longer visible or appears archived, submit a request through **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to retrieve your historical booking records.

rows.query: what size rental car am I allowed to rent for work
rows.answer:
You are allowed to book **up to intermediate size vehicles** (mini, economy, or compact) through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Exceptions that allow larger vehicles:**
- **Electric/hybrid vehicles:** If only standard or full-size electric/hybrid options are available, you may book those
- **3+ travelers** sharing the rental vehicle
- **Large work equipment** requiring additional cargo space
- **Pre-approved medical reasons**
- **Safety exceptions** (e.g., AWD/4WD in winter/blizzard-prone locations)

Standard, full-size cars, intermediate SUVs, or vans are permitted in the above situations. For full details, refer to [section 6.1 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#6).

rows.query: I need to move my flight to a different day, how do I change it
rows.answer:
## How to Modify Your Flight

You can change your flight using the **[Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)**:

1. Log in to the tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Select your segment and choose **Modify**
4. Choose the flight you want to change
5. Update your details and search for new flights
6. Complete checkout

For additional help, you can watch this video: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156).

> **Note:** If self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

rows.query: what kinds of flights am I actually allowed to book
rows.answer:
You're allowed to book the **Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class** that includes a seat assignment, using the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Key points:
- **Non-stop flights** are preferred over connecting flights when possible and practical.
- If a non-stop flight isn't the Lowest Logical Fare, consult your manager to find the right balance.
- **Preferred suppliers** listed in the booking tool are strongly encouraged, as they offer flexible terms, safety vetting, and additional traveler benefits.

**Upgrades:** Economy is the standard, but exceptions exist:
- If you qualify for the **Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program** (4+ one-way flights over 14 hours in the last 12 months), you can self-book premium economy for flights of 14 hours or more.
- You can also personally upgrade your fare class at any time, but you'll need to **pay the difference out of pocket** — Amazon only reimburses the policy-approved economy fare.

rows.query: how do I pick or change my seat on a flight
rows.answer:
## Selecting or Changing Your Seat

**During booking:**
- Seat maps are available during the shopping path to compare availability across flights.
- A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout, but you can choose a different seat by viewing the seat map before completing your booking.

**After booking:**
You can change your seat once ticketed by:
- Going to **Trips > My Trips** in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Using the **airline mobile app** directly (varies by airline)

**Note for EMEA and APAC travelers:** If a fare including a seat exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, purchase the seat separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense it separately, and save your receipt.

For a visual walkthrough, watch the quick video: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

rows.query: I have a medical condition, how do I request a travel accommodation
rows.answer:
Contact **Disability and Leave Services (DLS)** to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment or limitation.

**Key points:**
- **Only DLS can authorize** travel accommodations — your manager and Amazon Travel & Events cannot.
- Visit the [DLS site](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) for process details and requirements.
- If you are a **candidate or new hire**, contact the [Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/DLS-Applicant-Candidate-Accommodation/) instead.

You can also refer to **section 3.5 Travelers with Additional Needs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for further guidance.

rows.query: how do I set up my travel profile for the first time
rows.answer:
## Setting Up Your Travel Profile

To set up your Travel Profile for the first time:

1. **Go to the Self-Service Booking Tool:** [https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
2. **Create your profile**, which will securely store:
   - Personal information (mobile phone number, date of birth)
   - Passport details (for international travel)
   - Corporate or personal credit card (required for hotel reservations)
   - Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

> ⚠️ **Important:** Some information will be pre-populated — verify that your **name exactly matches your government-issued ID**.

---

### Quick Start Guides
Select the guide relevant to your region:
- **Global:** [Traveler Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/NewTravelSystem/)
- **India:** [Traveler Guide](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FProfile%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **China:** [Traveler Guide](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripSouce%20China%5FBCD%20Quick%20Start%20Guide%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **Japan:** [Quick Start

rows.query: do I get to keep my frequent flyer miles when I travel for work
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can keep any personal loyalty miles and points earned when traveling for business.

Here are the key things to know:

- Miles are earned through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** for airlines, hotels, and rental cars.
- To ensure you receive your miles, **add your loyalty program numbers to your Travel Profile**, making sure your name matches exactly.
- Miles are **automatically credited** to your personal account after each trip.
- If miles aren't credited, **contact the travel provider directly**.

**Note:** Employees based in **Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel** are **not eligible** to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax requirements.

rows.query: I got into a car accident in a rental during a work trip, what now
rows.answer:
## Steps to Follow After a Rental Car Accident on a Work Trip

Here's what you should do:

1. **Contact emergency services first** if there are any injuries or immediate safety concerns.

2. **Visit Amazon Travel Risk Management** (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) to follow the damage reporting process.

3. **Inform your manager** once the above steps are completed.

For full details, refer to **section 6.8 Car Accidents** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy: https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#62c58a98-3514-2159-75a6-abd568753d90

---

**Regarding insurance coverage:** If you booked through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** using a preferred supplier, Amazon provides **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)** and **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)** coverage. Note that deductibles may apply depending on the rental location. See section 6.2 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy for details.

rows.query: do I need a visa to travel internationally for business
rows.answer:
You must complete a **pre-travel immigration assessment** through the **International Business Travel Guided Experience** before traveling internationally. This assessment will determine your specific visa and immigration requirements based on your:

- **Destination country**
- **Citizenship**
- **Trip details** (duration and purpose)

For more information, refer to [Section 3.9 Visas and Passports](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: can I pay extra myself to upgrade on a really long flight
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can pay out of pocket to upgrade on a long flight.**

Amazon only reimburses the **Lowest Logical Fare in economy class** — you cover the difference yourself.

**How to do it (Global Access):**
1. In the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (desktop only), select your preferred upgraded cabin.
2. On the Out of Policy pop-up, select **Skip suggestions**.
3. At checkout, confirm your **personal card** is the form of payment and acknowledge the personal upgrade.
4. You pay the full amount upfront, then submit only the policy-approved portion for reimbursement.

> **Note:** If you're on a **regional** booking process, contact a Travel Counselor instead to request the personal upgrade.

**Also worth knowing for long flights:** If the flight is **14 hours or more** (excluding layovers), you may qualify for Amazon's **Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program**, which could allow you to book premium economy at company expense if you meet the eligibility criteria. Check your status via [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91).

rows.query: is it ok to add a few personal vacation days onto a work trip
rows.answer:
Yes, you can extend a business trip to add personal vacation days, but you must **obtain line manager approval before extending your trip**.

Keep in mind that Amazon will only reimburse costs associated with the **business portion** of the trip. Any personal expenses incurred during the extension are **your responsibility**.

For more details, refer to section 3.6 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need my manager to approve my travel before I book
rows.answer:
**No.** Manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Your manager will automatically receive a copy of your itinerary after you complete a booking, but this is **for awareness only** and requires no action from them.

rows.query: do I need to buy insurance for a rental car on a business trip
rows.answer:
**No, you do not need to purchase additional rental car insurance for business travel.**

Amazon provides the following coverage when you book through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) using preferred suppliers:

- **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)**
- **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)**

**At the rental counter:** Decline any additional insurance offered, unless it is legally required and not already included in your rate.

Note that deductibles may apply depending on the rental location.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: does the company provide travel insurance when I fly
rows.answer:
Yes. Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for business travel — you do **not** need to purchase separate travel insurance.

For details on coverage and how to file claims, visit the **Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program** page, or refer to **Section 10.4** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: the booking tool is broken, who do I contact for tech help
rows.answer: Go to **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to get technical support for the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool. You can use it to resolve login problems, system errors, payment display issues, or anything else preventing you from completing a booking.

rows.query: how do I reach a travel counselor or agent
rows.answer:
## Reaching a Travel Counselor

You can contact a Travel Counselor through the following methods:

- **Phone:** Use the number listed in your **Travel Profile** (this ensures proper call routing and identification). Find country-specific numbers on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).
- **Chat:** Available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries. *(Note: Chat does not support new reservations.)*
- **Email:** Also listed on the Travel & Events Wiki.

**Travel Counselors are available 24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries.

### Tips for faster service:
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- Ensure your **Travel Profile has your current phone number**
- If departing **within 72 hours**, you are automatically prioritized to the top of the call queue
- Use the **Callback Feature** to avoid waiting on hold

### Traveling internationally?
If you're outside your home country without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead of calling.

rows.query: can I book a personal vacation through the work travel tool
rows.answer:
**No.** The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) is for **business travel only**. Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business travel and are not available for personal trips.

For personal travel, visit **Amazon Extras** (extrasforamazon.com) for leisure travel promotions and personal booking options.

rows.query: what hotels am I allowed to book and what is the nightly limit
rows.answer:
## Hotel Booking Policy

You are allowed to book the **lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis**, close to your work destination. All bookings must be made through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or via a Travel Counselor.

**Amazon preferred hotels** are strongly encouraged as they offer competitive rates, flexible cancellation policies, loyalty program eligibility, and have been vetted for safety and security.

**Regarding a nightly rate limit** — I don't have that information in the available context. I'd recommend checking the [Global Travel and Expense Policy, Section 5.0](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) or contacting your Travel Counselor for specific rate cap details.

rows.query: why is the Amazon payment option not showing when I book a flight
rows.answer:
The Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution) is unavailable when your booking includes charges that are **not eligible for central billing**. Common reasons include:

- **Personal upgrades** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Seat selection fees**
- **Baggage fees**
- **Other ancillary charges**
- **Personal travel segments**

Additionally, some airlines require **multi-factor authentication** for payment processing, which also requires an alternate form of payment.

For any of these cases, you must use an **alternate payment method**.

If none of these conditions apply and you believe you should have access to the Amazon form of payment, contact **Amazon Travel Support** for assistance.

rows.query: I got a hotel confirmation email I never asked for, why
rows.answer:
This is expected behavior from Amazon Travel & Events' automated system. Here's what happened:

The system automatically monitors your hotel reservations for lower rates. When it found a better rate, it **automatically rebooked your hotel** to capture the savings.

**Nothing changed except:**
- Your confirmation number (you now have a new one)
- Your rate (it's lower)

Your property, dates, room type, and all other details remain the same.

**No action is needed from you**, but make sure to **use your new confirmation number** when checking in or contacting the hotel.

rows.query: I was charged for a hotel even though I cancelled it
rows.answer:
If you cancelled a hotel booking through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** and were still charged, contact **Amazon Travel Support** to investigate.

**Before contacting support, verify the following:**
- Cancellation was made within the hotel's cancellation period
- Date and time of cancellation
- Receipt from the hotel for the no-show charge

You can reach Amazon Travel Support at: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

rows.query: how do I cancel or change a hotel reservation
rows.answer:
## Cancelling or Modifying a Hotel Reservation

Use the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

### Before You Start, Confirm All of the Following:
- You have **not** checked into the hotel
- Your request is within the **cancellation policy guidelines**
- The hotel has **not** been paid for in advance

If your reservation doesn't meet these requirements, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

---

### How to Modify/Cancel:
1. Log in to the Self-Service Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Find your hotel reservation and select **Modify** or **Cancel**
4. Make your changes (dates, room type, etc.) and confirm

> **Tip:** If you need to book a completely different hotel, **book the new hotel first**, then cancel the old one — this prevents losing your reservation if the new hotel sells out.

---

For policy details, refer to **Section 3.10** of the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10).

rows.query: which expenses can I actually get reimbursed for
rows.answer:
Based on the policy context, here's a summary of what Amazon will reimburse:

**Travel**
- Airfare, hotel, car rental (must be booked through Amazon's TMC)
- Train, local transportation (taxis, Uber, Lyft), personal car mileage
- Business meals (individual or group) while traveling
- Laundry, tips/gratuities, passport/visa fees, hotel Wi-Fi

**Mobile & Communications**
- Up to **$50/month** for your personal mobile base plan (L4-L8)
- Up to **$150/month** for incremental travel-related mobile fees (roaming, hotspot, etc.)

**Work Equipment & Supplies**
- Office supplies, software, IT/general equipment below CAPEX threshold
- Virtual workspace equipment (monitor, chair, desk, headset, etc.)

**Professional Development**
- Training, seminars, conference registrations, professional dues/subscriptions

**Client & Business Engagement**
- Entertainment and meals with clients/vendors
- Gifts for clients (within policy limits)

**Key Reminders:**
- All expenses must reflect **business necessity and Frugality**
- Manager approval does **not** override policy
- Always check the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before purchasing to confirm eligibility
- Some expense types are region-specific or role-restricted

Would you like more detail on any specific expense category?

rows.query: what is the deadline to submit my expenses after a trip
rows.answer: You must submit your expense report within **60 days of the transaction date**. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses and corporate card transactions. Note that if you fail to submit corporate card transactions within 60 days, your corporate card may be suspended.

rows.query: how do I file an expense report
rows.answer:
## How to Submit an Expense Report

You can find step-by-step guidance in the user guides for whichever expense tool applies to you:

- **Concur:** [How to Submit Expenses - Concur User Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide)
- **Emburse:** [How to Submit Expenses - Emburse User Guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)

**Note:** Amazon is currently migrating from Concur to Emburse. Visit the **Expense Tool Migration Hub** to check the timeline for your location.

For additional help, you can also visit the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for video demos and how-to guides.

rows.query: my expense report got rejected, what do I do now
rows.answer:
## Your Expense Report Was Rejected

Here's what to do:

1. **Check the Expense Tool** at [expenses.corp.amazon.com](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) — your rejected report will be returned there with guidance on how to fix it.

2. **Review the feedback** — you may be asked to:
   - Submit legible receipts
   - Provide additional justification
   - Select a different expense type

3. **Fix and resubmit** — if you believe the rejection was an error, add a detailed justification in the **comment field** and resubmit.

4. **If additional approvals are needed** — your manager or skip-level may be added as a reviewer. No action is needed from you unless you want to follow up with them directly.

> **Note:** Resubmitting does not restart the 60-day submission deadline clock.

For additional help, contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: I can't figure out which expense category to use
rows.answer:
The **Expense Type Guide** is the best resource to help you find the right category. You can access it here:

- **Concur:** https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/
- **Emburse:** https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide

It contains detailed descriptions of every available expense type, including country-specific restrictions.

> **Note:** If you don't see a particular expense type in the Expense Tool, it likely isn't available based on your location.

If you'd like, share what type of expense you're trying to categorize and I can help point you in the right direction.

rows.query: are meals paid for while I'm traveling for business
rows.answer:
Yes, Amazon covers meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business. Here's what you need to know:

- **Receipts:** Retain the itemized receipt showing individual meal items to submit with your expense report.
- **Group meals:** The most senior person should pay and list all attendees when submitting. Only split the bill if each person will expense their own meal separately.
- **Government officials:** Additional rules apply if your meal includes a government official — review the Gift Reporting Guidelines and consult your manager before expensing.

For specific per diem rates and region-specific expense limits, visit the [Travel Reimbursement Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/).

rows.query: can I expense my cell phone bill
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can expense your cell phone bill** if you're an employee at levels L4–L8 (exceptions apply for L1–L3 and L10+).

**Here's how:**

- **Expense Type:** Select "Mobile Phone" in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/)
- **Enter** your total base plan cost and select your business use percentage:
  - 25% – infrequent use
  - 50% – mixed use
  - 75% – significant use
  - 100% – primarily work use
- **Reimbursement** is automatically calculated, **capped at $50/month**
- **Attach** your full itemized bill showing your name and/or phone number

**Note:** Only your **base plan** is covered. Additional lines, wearable device lines, or home internet are not included.

**Need to expense extra charges** (e.g., international roaming, mobile hotspot)? You can claim up to **$150/month** using the "Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees" expense type with detailed line-item documentation.

Review Section 12.6 of the [Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting to confirm your eligibility.

rows.query: how long until I actually get my reimbursement money
rows.answer:
Most expense reports are processed and paid within **4 business days**. However, it may take longer if your report:

- **Requires additional review** (if it's been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days, it's likely been flagged)
- **Requires additional approvals** from your manager or skip-level manager

To avoid delays, review the [Expense Report Checklist](https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) before submitting to ensure your report is complete, compliant, and accurate.

rows.query: I lost the receipt for something, can I still expense it
rows.answer:
Yes, you have three options if you're missing a receipt:

1. **Contact the merchant for a new receipt** – This is the preferred option and the **only acceptable solution** for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals.

2. **Submit a bank/card statement** – If you can't get a merchant receipt, submit a statement showing your name, transaction amount, and date (redact personal details and unrelated charges). This requires **manager approval**.

3. **Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD)** – A last resort for **low-value expenses only**. Not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete this yourself (delegates cannot) by checking "Missing Receipt" when creating the expense in the Expense Tool. Also requires **manager approval**, and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access.

rows.query: I accidentally paid for a personal thing on my corporate card
rows.answer:
**Here's what you need to do:**

1. **Find the charge** in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet.

2. **Select the Expense Type "Personal Expense"** (since the entire amount is personal/non-reimbursable).

3. **Fill out the required fields**, then build and submit your Expense Report.

**What happens next:** By submitting it as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or from the account you have set up in Amazon Touchstone.

> ⚠️ **Note:** Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension, so be careful going forward.

If you need help navigating the Expense Tool, refer to the [How to Submit Expenses user guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses).

rows.query: who do I talk to if I need help with an expense report
rows.answer:
You have several options for getting help with an expense report:

1. **Self-service**: Visit the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) to resolve common issues or be directed to the right team.
2. **Expense Tool guidance**: Search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for how-to guides and video demos.
3. **Community support**: Join the unofficial **#expenses-at-amazon-interest** Slack channel for advice from fellow Amazonians.
4. **Direct assistance**: Contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the [Employee Expenses & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: can I upgrade my fare using points or miles instead of paying
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can upgrade your fare using personal loyalty miles.** Here's how:

1. **Book your travel first** through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
2. **Contact the airline directly** to request the mileage upgrade — this cannot be done through the booking tool itself

**Important limitations to be aware of:**
- Not all airlines allow mileage upgrades for tickets booked through third-party platforms
- Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically **not eligible**
- Review your specific fare rules before requesting an upgrade

**Note:** Amazon only reimburses the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. Any upgrade cost (whether paid with money or miles) is a personal expense. Refer to section 3.12 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy for full details.

rows.query: why do I have to book through the official travel program at all
rows.answer:
Booking through Amazon Travel & Events is **required** for several key reasons:

1. **Safety & Security** – 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details, plus automatic registration for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.

2. **Cost Savings** – Access to exclusive corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are **not available** on consumer booking sites.

3. **Booking Simplicity** – Access to pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.

4. **Sustainability** – Supports Amazon's Climate Pledge commitment by offering lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotel options.

5. **Service Quality** – Pre-screened providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

Note that use of external booking methods is **strictly prohibited**, except in circumstances where no availability exists through the Travel Management Company (TMC).

rows.query: where is the contact info for travel support in my region
rows.answer:
You can find Travel Counselor contact information — including **phone, email, chat, and hours by country** — on the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki**:
👉 https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

**A few tips before you reach out:**
- Call from the phone number in your **Travel Profile** for proper routing
- Have your **Trip ID** ready for faster service
- If departing within **72 hours**, you'll be automatically prioritized in the call queue
- **Chat** is also available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (note: chat does not support new reservations)

If you're outside your home country without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead.

rows.query: what do I do if my flight gets cancelled by the airline
rows.answer:
Based on the available context, I don't have specific information about what to do when an airline cancels your flight.

However, the context does suggest that if **online self-service changes are not available** for your booking, you should:

- **Contact a Travel Counselor** at: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/
- Or visit the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki** for more information: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

You may also want to review **section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy: https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10

rows.query: are baggage fees and seat selection reimbursable when I travel
rows.answer:
**Yes, with conditions:**

**Standard policy:** You should book the **Lowest Logical Fare with seat assignment and baggage included**. When bundled this way, these costs are reimbursable.

**If you're in EMEA or APAC** and a fare that includes a standard seat or baggage exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits:
- Purchase the seat/baggage **separately** as an ancillary item directly with the airline
- Expense the additional cost separately
- Save all receipts

**Important exceptions — NOT reimbursable:**
- **Upgrade fees** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Premium seating fees**

When expensing, use the **"Airfare Fees"** expense type (GL code 66012) for charges incurred during approved air travel. Review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy, section 4.7](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) for the full list of reimbursable and non-reimbursable airfare fees.

rows.query: how do I book a flight and hotel for a business trip
rows.answer:
## Booking a Flight and Hotel for a Business Trip

Here's how to get started:

### 1. Set Up Your Travel Profile First
Before booking, you must create a Travel Profile at **https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/**. It will store your personal info, passport details, payment method, and travel preferences.

> **Important:** Verify your name exactly matches your government-issued ID.

### 2. Book Your Travel
- **Standard bookings:** Log in to the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights and hotels.
- **Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure):** Contact a **Travel Counselor** (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours.
- **Same-day travel outside the 6-hour window:** Book online through the Self-Service Booking Tool.

### 3. New to Amazon?
Review the **New Hire Travel Playbook** (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/newhire_playbook/) for a complete onboarding guide.

> **Note:** A corporate or personal credit card is required to secure hotel reservations.

rows.query: can I fly business class and how much will the company pay back
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can fly business class, but Amazon will only reimburse the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class.**

Here's how it works:

- **What Amazon pays:** The policy-approved economy fare (Lowest Logical Fare)
- **What you pay:** The difference between the economy fare and the business class fare, out of pocket

**How to book (Global Access users):**
1. Select your preferred business class fare in the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
2. On the Out of Policy pop-up, select *Skip suggestions*
3. At checkout, confirm your **personal card** is the payment method
4. Acknowledge the personal upgrade and complete the booking
5. You pay the full amount upfront, then expense only the policy-approved economy portion

**Note for Regional employees:** Contact a Travel Counselor directly to arrange the upgrade and obtain documentation showing the fare difference. Only the Lowest Logical Fare is reimbursable — upgrade and premium seating fees are **not** reimbursable.

Your manager will receive an email notification about the upgrade, but no action is required from them.

rows.query: what happens if my booking comes up as out of policy
rows.answer:
## Out of Policy Bookings

An **"Out of Policy"** flag means your selection exceeds policy guidelines, but **it does not prevent you from completing your booking**.

Here's what happens:

- **You will be prompted to provide a reason** for your selection — this is used as an audit tool.
- You **can proceed** with the booking if you have a valid business reason (e.g., a hotel closer to your meetings, or an SUV needed for safety in bad weather).

### Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation
| | Can Complete Booking? |
|---|---|
| **Out of Policy** | ✅ Yes — with a provided reason |
| **Policy Violation** (e.g., booking first class) | ❌ No — booking cannot be completed |

Simply provide a brief justification when prompted and proceed with your booking at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

rows.query: where do I find a trip I booked a few weeks ago
rows.answer:
You can find your previously booked trips by logging into the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and selecting **Trips** (or **Trips Library**, depending on your region) from the main menu. This gives you access to both current and past trips, including invoices.

If the trip is no longer visible or has been archived, submit a request through **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records.

rows.query: what size rental car am I allowed to rent for work
rows.answer:
You are allowed to book **up to intermediate size vehicles** (mini, economy, or compact) through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool.

**Exceptions that allow a larger vehicle:**
- **Electric/hybrid vehicles** – If only standard or full-size electric/hybrid options are available, you may book those
- **3 or more travelers** sharing the vehicle
- **Large work equipment** requiring extra cargo space
- **Pre-approved medical reasons**
- **Safety reasons** (e.g., AWD/4WD in winter/blizzard-prone areas)

For full details, refer to section 6.1 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: I need to move my flight to a different day, how do I change it
rows.answer:
## Modifying Your Flight

You can change your flight using the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Steps to modify your flight:**
1. Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Select your segment and choose **Modify**
4. Choose the flight you want to change
5. Update your details and search for new flights
6. Complete checkout

If self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a **Travel Counselor** for assistance.

For additional guidance, you can also watch this video: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156).

rows.query: what kinds of flights am I actually allowed to book
rows.answer:
You must book the **Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class** that includes a seat assignment, using the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Key points:
- **Non-stop flights** are preferred over connecting flights when possible and practical
- If a non-stop flight is **not** the Lowest Logical Fare, consult your manager to find the right balance between frugality and well-being
- **Premium Economy** is only available if you qualify for the PEA program (requires 4+ one-way flights over 14 hours in the last 12 months)

For full details, refer to section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: how do I pick or change my seat on a flight
rows.answer:
## Selecting or Changing Your Seat

**During booking:**
- Seat maps are available during the shopping path to compare availability across flights.
- A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout, but you can choose a different seat by viewing the seat map before completing your booking.

**After booking:**
You can change your seat once ticketed by:
- Navigating to **Trips > My Trips** in the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Using the **airline mobile app** directly (varies by airline)

**EMEA/APAC travelers note:** If a fare including a seat exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, purchase the seat separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense it separately, and save your receipt.

For a visual walkthrough, watch the [How to Change Your Seat video](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533).

rows.query: I have a medical condition, how do I request a travel accommodation
rows.answer:
## Requesting Medical Travel Accommodation

Contact **Disability and Leave Services (DLS)** to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment or limitation.

**Key points to know:**
- **Only DLS can authorize** travel accommodations — Amazon Travel & Events and your manager cannot approve these requests
- Visit the [DLS site](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) for information on the process and requirements

**If you are a Candidate or New Hire**, contact the **Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)** instead.

You can also refer to **section 3.5 Travelers with Additional Needs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for further details.

rows.query: how do I set up my travel profile for the first time
rows.answer:
## Setting Up Your Travel Profile

To set up your Travel Profile for the first time:

1. **Go to the Self-Service Booking Tool** at https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/
2. **Create your profile**, which will store:
   - Personal information (mobile phone number and date of birth)
   - Passport details (for international travel)
   - Corporate or personal credit card (required for hotel reservations)
   - Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

> ⚠️ **Important:** Some information will be pre-populated — verify that your **name exactly matches your government-issued ID**.

---

### Quick Start Guides (select your region):
- **Global Access travelers:** [Traveler Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/NewTravelSystem/)
- **India travelers:** [Traveler Guide (India)](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FProfile%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **China travelers:** [Traveler Guide (China)](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripSouce%20China%5FBCD%20Quick%20Start%20Guide%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **Japan travelers:** [Quick Start Videos (Japan)](https://broadcast.amazon.

rows.query: do I get to keep my frequent flyer miles when I travel for work
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can earn and keep personal loyalty miles when traveling for business.

Here's what you need to know:

- Miles apply to **airlines, hotels, and rental cars** booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).
- Miles are **automatically credited** to your personal account after each trip.
- Make sure your **loyalty program numbers are added to your Travel Profile** and that your name matches exactly.

**Note:** Employees based in **Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel** are not eligible due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

For more details, refer to **section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: I got into a car accident in a rental during a work trip, what now
rows.answer:
Here's what you need to do:

1. **Contact emergency services first** if there are injuries or immediate safety concerns.
2. **Visit Amazon Travel Risk Management** (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) to follow the damage reporting process.
3. **Inform your manager** once those steps are completed.

For full details, refer to **section 6.8 Car Accidents** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#62c58a98-3514-2159-75a6-abd568753d90).

**Regarding insurance coverage:** If you booked through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool using a preferred supplier, Amazon provides Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) coverage. Note that deductibles may apply depending on your rental location. See **section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** for more details.

rows.query: do I need a visa to travel internationally for business
rows.answer:
Yes, you may need a visa, but the specific requirements depend on your destination, citizenship, and trip details. You must complete a **pre-travel immigration assessment** through the **International Business Travel Guided Experience** before traveling internationally — this assessment will determine your specific visa and immigration requirements.

For more details, refer to **section 3.9 Visas and Passports** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy: https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9

rows.query: can I pay extra myself to upgrade on a really long flight
rows.answer:
Yes, you can pay to upgrade on a long flight, but Amazon only reimburses the **Lowest Logical Fare in economy class**. You would need to pay the difference out of pocket.

However, for very long flights, you may also want to check if you qualify for the **Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program**, which allows eligible travelers to book premium economy at Amazon's expense for flights **14 hours or more** (excluding layovers). You qualify automatically if you've completed **4+ one-way trips over 14 hours in the last 12 months** and book through Amazon Travel. You can check your PEA status via [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91).

If you don't qualify for PEA and want to upgrade at your own expense, the process depends on your booking access (Regional or Global Access) — but in both cases, you pay the full upgraded fare upfront and only expense the policy-approved economy portion.

rows.query: is it ok to add a few personal vacation days onto a work trip
rows.answer:
Yes, you can extend business trips to add personal time, but you need **line manager approval before extending your trip**.

Key points to note:
- Amazon reimburses **only the business-related costs**
- Any personal expenses during the extension are **your responsibility**

For more details, refer to section 3.6 Extended Trip for Personal Time in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need my manager to approve my travel before I book
rows.answer:
**No**, — manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings made through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Your manager will automatically receive a copy of your itinerary once the booking is complete, but this is **for awareness only** and requires no action from them.

rows.query: do I need to buy insurance for a rental car on a business trip
rows.answer:
**No, you do not need to purchase additional rental car insurance for business travel.**

Amazon automatically provides the following coverage when you book through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** and use preferred suppliers:

- **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)**
- **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)**

Note that deductibles may apply depending on the rental location.

**At the rental counter:** Decline any additional insurance offered, unless it is legally required and not already included in your rate.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: does the company provide travel insurance when I fly
rows.answer:
Yes. Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for business travel — you do **not** need to purchase separate travel insurance.

For full details on coverage, loss/damage guidelines, and how to file claims, visit the [Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program](https://inside.hr.amazon.dev/content/inside/us/en/employment/your-career-global/insurance-and-assistance-program.html) page, or refer to **section 10.4** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: the booking tool is broken, who do I contact for tech help
rows.answer: Access **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to get technical support for the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool. You can use it to resolve login problems, system errors, payment display issues, or anything else preventing you from completing a booking.

rows.query: how do I reach a travel counselor or agent
rows.answer:
## Reaching a Travel Counselor

Here are your options for contacting a Travel Counselor:

- **Phone:** Call the number listed in your **Travel Profile** (this ensures proper call routing and identification). Travel Counselors are available **24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries.
- **Chat:** Available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries. *(Note: Chat does not support new reservations.)*
- **Email:** Also available as a contact option.

For full contact details (phone, email, chat, and hours by country), visit the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).

### Tips for faster service:
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- Ensure your **Travel Profile has your current phone number**
- If departing **within 72 hours**, you are automatically prioritized to the top of the call queue
- Use the **Callback Feature** if you don't want to wait on hold

### If you're traveling internationally:
If you're outside your home country without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead of calling.

rows.query: can I book a personal vacation through the work travel tool
rows.answer:
**No.** The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) is for **business travel only**. Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business travel and are not available for personal trips.

For personal travel, visit **Amazon Extras** (https://extrasforamazon.com/app/home) for leisure travel promotions and personal booking options.

rows.query: what hotels am I allowed to book and what is the nightly limit
rows.answer:
## Hotel Booking Guidelines

You are allowed to book the **lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis**, close to your work destination, through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or via a Travel Counselor.

**Regarding a nightly rate limit** — I don't have that information in the available context. You may want to check the [Global Travel and Expense Policy Section 5.0](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) or contact your Travel Counselor for details on any nightly rate caps.

### Other key points:
- **Preferred hotels** are recommended as they offer competitive rates, flexible cancellation, and have been vetted for safety/security.
- **Long-term stays (7+ nights):** Consider Corporate Serviced Apartments (studio or one-bedroom, whichever is less expensive) — requires **L8+ approval**.
- **Airbnb/shared economy properties** are strongly discouraged and should only be used when no hotel or serviced apartment is available.

rows.query: why is the Amazon payment option not showing when I book a flight
rows.answer:
The Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution) is unavailable when your booking includes charges not eligible for central billing. Common reasons include:

- **Personal upgrades** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Seat selection fees**
- **Baggage fees**
- **Other ancillary charges**
- **Personal travel segments**

Additionally, some airlines require multi-factor authentication for payment processing, which also requires an alternate form of payment.

For any of these cases, you'll need to use an alternate payment method.

If none of these conditions apply and you believe you should have access, contact [Amazon Travel Support](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

rows.query: I got a hotel confirmation email I never asked for, why
rows.answer:
## Automated Hotel Rebooking

You received a new confirmation because **Amazon Travel & Events' automated auditing system found a lower rate** for your existing reservation and automatically rebooked it to capture the savings.

**What stays the same:**
- Hotel property
- Check-in/check-out dates
- Room type and preferences

**What changes:**
- Your confirmation number (the new one you just received)
- Your rate (it's now lower)

**No action is needed from you.** Just make sure to use your **new confirmation number** when checking in or contacting the hotel.

rows.query: I was charged for a hotel even though I cancelled it
rows.answer:
If you cancelled your hotel booking through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** and were still charged, contact **Amazon Travel Support** to investigate.

**Before reaching out, make sure you have the following ready:**
- Confirmation that your cancellation was within the hotel's cancellation period
- The date and time of your cancellation
- Receipt from the hotel for the no-show charge

You can reach Amazon Travel Support at: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

rows.query: how do I cancel or change a hotel reservation
rows.answer:
## Canceling or Modifying a Hotel Reservation

Use the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

### Before You Start, Confirm:
- You have **not** already checked in
- Your request is within the **cancellation policy guidelines**
- The hotel was **not paid in advance**

If any of these conditions apply, contact a **Travel Counselor** for assistance.

---

### To Modify Your Hotel:
1. Log in to the Self-Service Booking Tool
2. Go to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Find your hotel reservation and select **Modify** (or **Cancel**)
4. Update check-in/check-out dates or select a different room type/rate
5. Review changes and any price differences, then select **Book Hotel** to confirm

> **Note (Regional users):** If you need entirely new dates or a different hotel, **book the new reservation first**, then cancel the old one — this ensures you don't lose your current booking if the new hotel sells out.

---

For policy details, refer to **Section 3.10** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: which expenses can I actually get reimbursed for
rows.answer:
Based on the policy, reimbursable expenses are those that are **approved, necessary to your job, and reflect good judgment and Amazon's Frugality principle**.

Common reimbursable expense categories include:

- **Travel**: Airfare, hotel, car rental, train, local transportation (Uber/Lyft, taxis), personal mileage
- **Meals**: Individual travel meals, group business meals, tips/gratuities
- **Mobile/Communications**: Up to **$50/month** for your mobile base plan; up to **$150/month** for incremental travel-related mobile fees (roaming, hotspots, etc.)
- **Travel incidentals**: Laundry, passport/visa fees, currency exchange fees, parking, hotel Wi-Fi
- **Office/Equipment**: Office supplies, software, equipment below CAPEX threshold
- **Training & Development**: Seminars, courses, professional dues/subscriptions
- **Client/Vendor Engagement**: Client entertainment, external gifts (within policy limits)

**Important caveats:**
- Always **consult the Global Travel and Expense Policy** before purchasing
- Some expense types are **region-specific** or **role-restricted**
- **Manager or PXT approval does not override policy**
- Switzerland-based employees must also follow the Switzerland-specific T&E policy

For a full list, review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) or visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: what is the deadline to submit my expenses after a trip
rows.answer: You must submit your expense report within **60 days of the transaction date**. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses and corporate card transactions. Note that if you miss the 60-day deadline for corporate card transactions, your corporate card may be suspended.

rows.query: how do I file an expense report
rows.answer:
## Submitting an Expense Report

You can submit an expense report through one of two tools, depending on your location:

- **Concur:** https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/
- **Emburse:** https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com

Check the **Expense Tool Migration Hub** to find out which tool applies to your location.

**Step-by-step guides are available here:**
- [Concur User Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide)
- [Emburse User Guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)

**Reminder:** Expense reports must be submitted **within 60 days** of the transaction date to avoid corporate card suspension.

rows.query: my expense report got rejected, what do I do now
rows.answer:
## Your Expense Report Was Rejected

Here's what to do:

1. **Check the Expense Tool** (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) — Your rejected report will be returned there with **guidance on how to fix and resubmit** it.

2. **Common fixes you may be asked to make:**
   - Submit legible receipts
   - Provide additional justification
   - Select a different expense type

3. **If you think it was rejected in error:** Review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) and add a **detailed justification in the comment field** before resubmitting.

4. **If additional approvals are needed:** Your manager or skip-level may be added as a reviewer — no action is required from you unless you want to follow up with them directly.

> **Need further help?** Contact the Amazon Global Expense Team through the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: I can't figure out which expense category to use
rows.answer:
To find the right expense type, refer to the **Expense Type Guide**:

- **Concur users:** https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/
- **Emburse users:** https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide

The guide contains detailed descriptions of every available expense type, including country-specific restrictions.

If you don't see the expense type you need in the Expense Tool, that expense likely isn't available based on your location.

If you'd like help with a **specific expense**, share what you're trying to submit and I can help point you to the right category.

rows.query: are meals paid for while I'm traveling for business
rows.answer:
Yes, meals are covered during business travel. Amazon reimburses meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business.

**Key requirements:**
- Retain **itemized receipts** showing individual meal items to submit with your expense report
- Use the **"Individual Travel Meal"** expense type for solo meals while traveling
- For **group meals**, the most senior person should pay and list all attendees when submitting

**Additional notes:**
- Specific per diem limits and regional guidance can be found in the [Travel Reimbursement Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/)
- **Germany employees** must submit for a tax-free per diem instead of expensing individual meals
- If your meal includes a **government official**, additional rules apply — review the Gift Reporting Guidelines and consult your manager before submitting

rows.query: can I expense my cell phone bill
rows.answer:
**Yes, your mobile phone bill is reimbursable** (for employees L4-L8).

**How it works:**
- Amazon reimburses up to **$50 USD/month** for your personal mobile phone **base plan**
- When submitting in the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/), select **"Mobile Phone"** as the Expense Type
- Enter your total base plan cost and select your business use percentage:
  - 25% – infrequent use
  - 50% – mixed use
  - 75% – significant use
  - 100% – primarily work use
- The system automatically calculates your reimbursement (capped at $50)
- You must attach a **full itemized bill** showing your name and/or phone number

**Additional travel-related mobile charges:**
If you incur extra work-related costs (international roaming, mobile hotspots, etc.), you can claim up to **$150/month** using the **"Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees"** Expense Type with detailed line-item documentation.

**Note:** Exceptions apply for L1-3 and L10+. Review [Section 12.6 of the Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting.

rows.query: how long until I actually get my reimbursement money
rows.answer:
Most expense reports are processed and paid within **4 business days**. However, it may take longer if your report:

- **Has been flagged** for additional review (likely if it's been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days)
- **Requires additional approvals** from your manager or skip-level manager

To avoid delays, review the [Expense Report Checklist](https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) before submitting to ensure your report is complete, compliant, and accurate.

rows.query: I lost the receipt for something, can I still expense it
rows.answer:
Yes, you have three options if you're missing a receipt:

1. **Contact the merchant for a new receipt** – This is the preferred option and the **only acceptable solution** for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals.

2. **Submit a bank/card statement** – If you can't get a merchant receipt, provide a statement showing your name, transaction amount, and date (redact personal details and unrelated charges). This requires **manager approval**.

3. **Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD)** – A last resort for **low-value expenses only**. Not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete this yourself (delegates cannot) by checking "Missing Receipt" when creating the expense in the Expense Tool. This also requires **manager approval**, and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access.

rows.query: I accidentally paid for a personal thing on my corporate card
rows.answer:
**Here's what to do:**

1. Find the corporate card charge in the **Expense Tool** under your eWallet.
2. Select the Expense Type **"Personal Expense"** (if the entire amount is personal).
3. Fill out the required fields.
4. Submit the expense report.

**What happens next:** By submitting as a personal expense, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or your account in Amazon Touchstone.

⚠️ **Note:** Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in **card suspension**, so be careful going forward.

If you only need to mark **part** of the transaction as personal, you can split it by adding an allocation for "PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense" and adjusting the amounts accordingly.

rows.query: who do I talk to if I need help with an expense report
rows.answer:
You have several options for help with an expense report:

1. **Self-service**: Visit the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) to solve common issues or be directed to the right team.
2. **Expense Tool guidance**: Search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for video demos and how-to guides.
3. **Peer support**: Join the unofficial **#expenses-at-amazon-interest** Slack channel for advice from fellow Amazonians.
4. **Direct support**: Contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the [Employee Expenses & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: can I upgrade my fare using points or miles instead of paying
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can upgrade your fare using personal loyalty miles, but with some important limitations:

- After booking through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool**, you must **contact the airline directly** to request the mileage upgrade — it cannot be processed through the booking tool itself.
- **Not all airlines allow** mileage upgrades on tickets purchased through third-party booking platforms.
- **Basic economy and deeply discounted fares** are typically ineligible for mileage upgrades.
- You should review your specific fare rules before requesting an upgrade.

Refer to **section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for more details.

rows.query: why do I have to book through the official travel program at all
rows.answer:
Booking through Amazon Travel & Events is **required** for several key reasons:

1. **Safety & Security** – 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details, plus automatic registration for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.

2. **Cost Savings** – Access to exclusive corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are **not available** on consumer booking sites.

3. **Booking Simplicity** – Access to pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.

4. **Sustainability** – Supports Amazon's Climate Pledge commitment by offering lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotel options.

5. **Service Quality** – Pre-screened providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

Note: Using external booking methods is **strictly prohibited**, except when no availability exists through the Travel Management Company (TMC).

rows.query: where is the contact info for travel support in my region
rows.answer:
You can find Travel Counselor contact information — including **phone, email, chat, and hours by country** — on the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki**:
👉 https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

**A few tips before you contact them:**
- Call from the phone number in your **Travel Profile** for proper routing
- Have your **Trip ID** ready for faster service
- If departing within **72 hours**, you're automatically prioritized in the call queue
- **Chat** is available within the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool, but does not support new reservations
- If you're outside your home country without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead

rows.query: what do I do if my flight gets cancelled by the airline
rows.answer:
Based on the available context, I don't have specific information about what to do when an airline cancels your flight.

However, the context does suggest that if **online self-service changes are not available** for your booking, you should **contact a Travel Counselor** for assistance: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

You can also refer to **section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for further guidance: https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10

rows.query: are baggage fees and seat selection reimbursable when I travel
rows.answer:
**Yes, but with conditions:**

- **Book the Lowest Logical Fare** that includes a seat assignment and baggage when possible — this is the preferred approach.

- **If you're in EMEA or APAC** and a fare that includes a seat/baggage exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, you should:
  - Purchase the seat or baggage separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline
  - Expense the additional cost separately
  - Save all receipts

- **Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are NOT reimbursable.** Amazon only reimburses up to the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class — any difference for upgraded seating must be paid out of pocket.

For full details, refer to section **4.7 Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: how do I book a flight and hotel for a business trip
rows.answer:
## Booking a Flight and Hotel for a Business Trip

Here's how to get started:

**1. Set Up Your Travel Profile First**
Before booking, you must create a Travel Profile at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). It will store your personal info, passport details, and payment method.

> ⚠️ Verify your name exactly matches your government-issued ID.

**2. Book Your Travel**
Once your profile is set up:
- Log in to the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Search for **flights and hotels** directly through the tool

**3. Last-Minute Bookings**
- **Within 6 hours of departure:** Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours
- **Same-day travel (outside 6-hour window):** Book online through the Self-Service Tool

**New to Amazon?**
Review the [New Hire Travel Playbook](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/newhire_playbook/) for a complete onboarding guide.

For policy details, refer to **section 3.3 How to Book Travel** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: can I fly business class and how much will the company pay back
rows.answer:
## Flying Business Class

**Yes, you can upgrade to business class**, but Amazon will only reimburse the **Lowest Logical Fare in economy class**. You are responsible for paying the difference out of pocket.

### How reimbursement works:
- You pay the **full ticket cost** upfront
- Amazon reimburses only the **policy-approved economy fare**
- The personal upgrade cost is **your expense**

### How to book (Global Access users):
1. Select your preferred upgraded cabin in the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (desktop only)
2. The fare indicator will show you the policy-approved amount vs. your personal upgrade cost
3. On the Out of Policy pop-up, select **"Skip suggestions"**
4. Confirm your **personal card** is the payment method
5. Acknowledge the personal upgrade and book

Your itinerary will include a **Personal Upgrade Summary** to use when submitting your expense report for the reimbursable portion.

> **Note:** Your manager will receive an email notification about the upgrade, but no action is required from them.

For the exact policy-approved amount, the booking tool will display this at the time of purchase since it varies by trip.

rows.query: what happens if my booking comes up as out of policy
rows.answer:
## Out of Policy Bookings

An **"Out of Policy"** flag identifies options that don't comply with the Global Travel and Expense Policy, but **it does not prevent you from completing your booking**.

### Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation
- **Out of Policy**: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but can be justified for business reasons — you **can proceed** but must provide a reason.
- **Policy Violation**: Your selection breaks a hard rule (e.g., booking first class) — the booking **cannot be completed**.

### When You Can Proceed
You may book out of policy if there's a valid business reason, such as:
- A hotel closer to your meetings
- An SUV during bad weather for safety

When prompted, simply **provide a brief justification**. This reason is used as an audit tool to balance flexibility with policy compliance.

rows.query: where do I find a trip I booked a few weeks ago
rows.answer:
You can find your previously booked trips in the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/):

- **Global Access users**: Select **Trips** from the main menu to access current and past trips, including invoices.
- **Regional users**: Select **Trips Library** from the main menu.

If the trip is no longer visible or has been archived, open a request through **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records.

rows.query: what size rental car am I allowed to rent for work
rows.answer:
You are allowed to book **up to intermediate size vehicles** (mini, economy, or compact) through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Exceptions allowing larger vehicles (standard, full-size, intermediate SUV, or van):**
- 3 or more travelers sharing the vehicle
- Large work equipment requiring extra cargo space
- Pre-approved medical reasons
- Safety exceptions (e.g., AWD/4WD in winter/blizzard-prone areas)

**Note on electric/hybrid vehicles:** If the only available electric or hybrid options are standard or full-size, you may book those instead of an intermediate.

rows.query: I need to move my flight to a different day, how do I change it
rows.answer:
## How to Modify Your Flight

You can change your flight using the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Steps to modify your flight:**
1. Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Select your segment and choose **Modify**
4. Choose the flight you want to change
5. Update your details and search for new flights
6. Complete checkout

If self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a **Travel Counselor** for assistance.

You can also watch this quick video for guidance: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156)

rows.query: what kinds of flights am I actually allowed to book
rows.answer:
You are allowed to book the **Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class** that includes a seat assignment, booked through the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Key points:
- **Non-stop flights** are preferred over connecting flights when possible and practical.
- If a non-stop flight is **not** the Lowest Logical Fare, consult your manager to find the right balance.
- **Business travel only** — personal travel cannot be booked through the tool.
- **Preferred suppliers** listed in the booking tool are strongly encouraged for safety, flexible terms, and added benefits.

**Upgrades:** You may upgrade at your own expense (paying the difference out of pocket), or qualify for **Premium Economy** through the PEA program if you've completed 4+ one-way flights over 14 hours in the past 12 months.

rows.query: how do I pick or change my seat on a flight
rows.answer:
## Selecting or Changing Your Seat

**During booking:**
- Seat maps are available during the shopping path to compare availability across flights.
- A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout, but you can view the seat map and choose a different seat before completing your booking.

**After booking:**
You can change your seat once ticketed by:
- Navigating to **Trips > My Trips** in the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Using the **airline mobile app** directly (varies by airline)

> 📹 For a walkthrough, watch: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

**Note for EMEA/APAC travelers:** If a fare including a seat assignment exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, purchase the seat separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense it separately, and save your receipt.

rows.query: I have a medical condition, how do I request a travel accommodation
rows.answer:
To request a travel accommodation due to a medical condition or disability-related impairment:

1. **Contact Disability and Leave Services (DLS)** — only DLS can authorize travel accommodations. Your manager and Amazon Travel & Events **cannot** approve these requests.

2. **Visit the DLS site** for process details and requirements.

3. **New hire or candidate?** Contact the **Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)** instead.

For additional policy details, refer to **section 3.5 Travelers with Additional Needs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: how do I set up my travel profile for the first time
rows.answer:
## Setting Up Your Travel Profile

To set up your Travel Profile for the first time:

1. **Go to the Self-Service Booking Tool:** [https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
2. **Create your profile**, which will store:
   - Personal information (mobile phone number, date of birth)
   - Passport details (for international travel)
   - Corporate or personal credit card (required for hotel reservations)
   - Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

**Important:** Some information will be pre-populated — always verify that your **name exactly matches your government-issued ID**.

---

### Quick Start Guides
Select the guide relevant to your region:
- **Global/Regional:** [Traveler Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/NewTravelSystem/)
- **India:** [Traveler Guide (India)](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FProfile%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **Japan:** [Quick Start Videos (Japan)](https://broadcast.amazon.com/channels/1303/playlists/18326)
- **China:** [Traveler Guide (China)](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripSouce%20China%5FBCD%20Quick%20Start%20Guide%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2F
