rows.query: do I get to keep my frequent flyer miles when I travel for work
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can keep any personal loyalty miles and points earned while traveling for business.

Here are the key points to know:

- This applies to **airlines, hotels, and rental cars** booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Miles are **automatically credited** to your personal account after each trip
- Make sure your **loyalty numbers are added to your Travel Profile** and that your name matches exactly between your profile and loyalty program

**Exception:** Employees based in **Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel** are not eligible due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

rows.query: I got into a car accident in a rental during a work trip, what now
rows.answer:
Here's what you need to do:

1. **Contact emergency services first** if there are any injuries or immediate safety concerns.

2. **Report the damage** by visiting [Amazon Travel Risk Management](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) for the damage reporting process.

3. **Inform your manager** once the above steps are completed.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.8 Car Accidents** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

**Regarding insurance coverage:** If you booked through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool using a preferred supplier, Amazon provides both Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) coverage, though deductibles may apply depending on the rental location. See **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for more details.

rows.query: do I need a visa to travel internationally for business
rows.answer:
You may need a visa, but the specific requirements depend on your destination, citizenship, and trip details. You are required to complete a **pre-travel immigration assessment** through the **International Business Travel Guided Experience** before traveling internationally. This assessment will determine your specific visa and immigration requirements.

For more details, refer to [Section 3.9 Visas and Passports](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: can I pay extra myself to upgrade on a really long flight
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you have two options for upgrading on a long flight:

1. **Pay out of pocket (personal upgrade):** You can upgrade to a higher cabin class, but Amazon only reimburses the Lowest Logical Fare in economy. You pay the difference yourself. When booking, select your preferred cabin in the Self-Service Booking Tool, acknowledge the personal upgrade, and ensure your personal card is set as the payment method.

2. **Premium Economy (PEA program):** If your flight is **14 hours or more** (excluding layovers), you may qualify for Amazon's Premium Economy Accommodation program, which allows you to book premium economy at no personal cost. You automatically qualify once you've completed **4+ one-way trips over 14 hours in the past 12 months** — no action needed on your part. To check your status, open a request through [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91).

> **Note:** When booking a personal upgrade, you won't be able to use the Amazon form of payment — you'll need to use your own payment method for the full amount upfront.

rows.query: is it ok to add a few personal vacation days onto a work trip
rows.answer:
Yes, you can extend a business trip to add personal vacation days, but you need **line manager approval before extending your trip**.

Keep in mind:
- **Amazon only reimburses the business portion** of the trip
- **All personal expenses during the extension are your responsibility**

For more details, refer to section 3.6 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need my manager to approve my travel before I book
rows.answer:
**No**, — manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Your manager will automatically receive a copy of your itinerary once the booking is complete, but this is **for awareness only** and requires no action from them.

rows.query: do I need to buy insurance for a rental car on a business trip
rows.answer:
**No, you do not need to purchase additional rental car insurance for business trips.**

Amazon already provides:
- **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)**
- **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)**

for business rentals booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) using preferred suppliers. Note that deductibles may apply depending on the rental location.

**At the rental counter:** Decline any additional insurance offered, unless it is legally required and not already included in your rate.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: does the company provide travel insurance when I fly
rows.answer:
Yes. Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for all business travel — you do **not** need to purchase separate travel insurance.

For details on coverage and how to file claims, visit the **Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program** page, or refer to **section 10.4** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: the booking tool is broken, who do I contact for tech help
rows.answer: Go to **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to get technical support for the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool. You can use it to resolve login problems, system errors, payment display issues, or anything else preventing you from completing a booking.

rows.query: how do I reach a travel counselor or agent
rows.answer:
## Reaching a Travel Counselor

You can contact a Travel Counselor through the following methods:

- **Phone:** Call the number listed in your **Travel Profile** (this ensures proper call routing and identification). Find country-specific numbers on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).
- **Chat:** Available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries. *(Note: Chat does not support new reservations.)*
- **Email:** Also available — details by country on the Travel & Events Wiki.

**Travel Counselors are available 24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries.

### Tips for faster service:
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- Ensure your **Travel Profile has your current phone number**
- If departing **within 72 hours**, you are automatically prioritized to the top of the call queue
- Use the **Callback Feature** to avoid waiting on hold

### Traveling internationally?
If you're outside your home country without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead of calling.

rows.query: can I book a personal vacation through the work travel tool
rows.answer:
**No.** The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) is for **business travel only**. Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business travel and are not available for personal trips.

For personal travel, visit **Amazon Extras** (extrasforamazon.com) for leisure travel promotions and personal booking options.

rows.query: what hotels am I allowed to book and what is the nightly limit
rows.answer:
## Hotel Booking Guidelines

You are allowed to book the **lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis** near your work destination, booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or a Travel Counselor.

**Amazon preferred hotels** are strongly encouraged as they offer competitive rates, flexible cancellation policies, loyalty program eligibility, and have been vetted for safety and security.

---

Regarding a **nightly rate limit**, I don't have that information in the available context. I'd recommend checking the [Global Travel and Expense Policy Section 5.0](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) or contacting a Travel Counselor for details on any nightly caps.

rows.query: why is the Amazon payment option not showing when I book a flight
rows.answer:
The Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution) is unavailable when your booking includes charges that are **not eligible for central billing**. Common reasons include:

- **Personal upgrades** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Seat selection fees**
- **Baggage fees**
- **Other ancillary charges**
- **Personal travel segments**
- **Multi-factor authentication required by some airlines** (also requires an alternate payment method)

For any of these situations, you'll need to use an **alternate payment method**.

If none of these conditions apply and you believe you should have access, contact **Amazon Travel Support** at https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/ for assistance.

rows.query: I got a hotel confirmation email I never asked for, why
rows.answer:
This is expected behavior from Amazon Travel & Events' automated auditing system. It monitors your hotel reservations after booking and **automatically rebooks you at a lower rate** when one becomes available.

**What stays the same:** hotel property, check-in/check-out dates, room type, and all other details.

**What changes:** your confirmation number and your rate (it will be lower).

**No action is needed from you**, but make sure to **use your new confirmation number** when checking in or contacting the hotel.

rows.query: I was charged for a hotel even though I cancelled it
rows.answer:
If you cancelled through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** and were still charged, contact **Amazon Travel Support** to investigate.

**Before reaching out, gather the following:**
- Confirmation that your cancellation was within the hotel's cancellation period
- Date and time of your cancellation
- Receipt from the hotel for the no-show charge

Then contact [Amazon Travel Support](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) to open an investigation.

rows.query: how do I cancel or change a hotel reservation
rows.answer:
## Cancelling or Modifying a Hotel Reservation

Use the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

### Before You Start, Confirm All of the Following:
- You have **not** checked into the hotel
- Your request is **within the cancellation policy** guidelines
- The hotel has **not been paid for in advance**

If your reservation doesn't meet these requirements, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

---

### How to Modify:
1. Log in to the Self-Service Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Find your hotel reservation and select **Modify** (or **Cancel**)
4. Update dates or room type as needed
5. Review any price differences and select **Book Hotel** to confirm

> **Tip (Regional users):** If booking new dates or a different hotel, **book the new reservation first**, then cancel the old one — this protects you in case your preferred hotel sells out.

---

For further help, refer to [Section 3.10 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10).

rows.query: which expenses can I actually get reimbursed for
rows.answer:
Based on the policy context, reimbursable business expenses are those that are **approved, necessary to your job, and reflect good judgment and Frugality**. Here are key categories with examples:

**Travel (Transportation & Lodging)**
- Airfare, hotel, car rental, train, local transportation (Uber/taxi)
- Must be booked through Amazon's Travel Management Company (TMC)

**Meals**
- Individual travel meals, group business meals, tips/gratuities during travel

**Mobile/Communications**
- Up to **$50/month** for personal mobile base plan (L4-L8)
- Up to **$150/month** for incremental travel-related mobile fees (roaming, hotspots, etc.)

**Travel Incidentals**
- Laundry, passport/visa fees, currency exchange fees, hotel Wi-Fi, business parking

**Other Common Expenses**
- Office supplies, training/professional development, corporate shipping, software (select countries)
- Virtual workspace equipment (chair, desk, monitor, headset, etc.)

**Important reminders:**
- Always consult the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) **before** making a purchase
- Manager approval alone does **not** make a non-policy expense reimbursable
- Switzerland-based employees must also follow the Switzerland-specific policy
- Non-Amazon employees (contractors, candidates) follow a separate Non-Employee policy

rows.query: what is the deadline to submit my expenses after a trip
rows.answer: You must submit your expense report within **60 days of the transaction date**. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses and corporate card transactions. Note that if you miss the 60-day deadline for corporate card transactions, your corporate card may be suspended.

rows.query: how do I file an expense report
rows.answer:
## Submitting an Expense Report

You can submit an expense report through one of two tools, depending on your location:

- **Emburse**: https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com
- **Concur**: https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/

*(Note: A migration from Concur to Emburse is currently underway — visit the Expense Tool Migration Hub to check the timeline for your location.)*

**Step-by-step guides are available here:**
- [Concur User Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide)
- [Emburse User Guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)

**Key reminders:**
- Submit expense reports **within 60 days** of the transaction date.
- Corporate card transactions will automatically appear in the Expense Tool within **5 business days**.
- Missing the 60-day deadline may result in **card suspension**.

For additional help, visit the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) or the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: my expense report got rejected, what do I do now
rows.answer:
## Your Expense Report Was Rejected

Here's what to do:

1. **Check the Expense Tool** at [expenses.corp.amazon.com](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) — your rejected report will be returned there with **guidance on how to fix and resubmit** it.

2. **Common fixes you may be asked to make:**
   - Submit legible receipts
   - Provide additional justification
   - Select a different expense type

3. **If you think it was rejected in error:** Review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication), then add a **detailed justification in the comment field** and resubmit.

4. **If additional approvals are needed:** Your manager or skip-level manager will be added as a reviewer — no action is required from you unless you want to follow up with them directly.

> **Need more help?** Contact the Amazon Global Expense Team through the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: I can't figure out which expense category to use
rows.answer:
Use the **Expense Type Guide** to find the right category:

- **Concur users:** https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/
- **Emburse users:** https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide

The guide contains detailed descriptions of every available expense type, including country-specific restrictions.

If you don't see the expense type you need in the Expense Tool, that expense likely isn't available based on your location.

rows.query: are meals paid for while I'm traveling for business
rows.answer:
Yes, meals are covered during business travel. Amazon reimburses meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business.

**Key things to keep in mind:**
- Retain your **itemized receipt** to submit with your expense report
- For **group/team meals**, the most senior person should pay and list all attendees when submitting
- Only split the bill if each person plans to expense their meal separately
- If a **government official** is present at the meal, additional rules apply — review the Gift Reporting Guidelines and consult your manager before expensing

For specific per diem rates and regional expense limits, visit the [Travel Reimbursement Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/).

rows.query: can I expense my cell phone bill
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — here's how it works:

**Who's eligible:** Employees at levels L4–L8 (exceptions apply for L1–L3 and L10+).

**Reimbursement cap:** Up to **$50 USD/month** for your personal mobile phone **base plan**.

**How to submit:**
1. Go to the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/)
2. Select **"Mobile Phone"** as the Expense Type
3. Enter your total base plan cost
4. Select your business use percentage:
   - 25% – infrequent use
   - 50% – mixed use
   - 75% – significant use
   - 100% – primarily work use
5. Attach your **full itemized bill** showing your name and/or phone number

The system will automatically calculate your reimbursement, capped at $50.

**Extra charges (roaming, hotspot, etc.):** You can also get reimbursed up to **$150/month** for incremental work-related mobile costs (e.g., international roaming, travel daily fees, mobile hotspots). Submit these using the **"Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees"** Expense Type with detailed line-item documentation.

Before submitting, review **Section 12.6** of the [Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) to confirm eligibility.

rows.query: how long until I actually get my reimbursement money
rows.answer:
Most expense reports are processed and paid within **4 business days**. However, it may take longer if:

- Your report has been **flagged for additional review** (likely if it's been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days)
- Your report **requires additional approvals** from your manager or skip-level manager

To avoid delays, review the [Expense Report Checklist](https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) before submitting.

rows.query: I lost the receipt for something, can I still expense it
rows.answer:
Yes, you have three options for expensing without a receipt:

1. **Contact the merchant for a new receipt** – This is the preferred option and the **only acceptable solution for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals**.

2. **Submit a bank/card statement** – If you can't get a merchant receipt, submit a statement showing your name, transaction amount, and date (redact personal details and unrelated charges). This requires **manager approval**.

3. **Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD)** – A last resort for **low-value expenses only**. Not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete this yourself (delegates cannot) by checking "Missing Receipt" when creating the expense in the Expense Tool. This also requires **manager approval**, and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access.

rows.query: I accidentally paid for a personal thing on my corporate card
rows.answer:
**No worries — here's what to do:**

1. Open the **Expense Tool** and find the corporate card charge in your **eWallet**.
2. Select the Expense Type **"Personal Expense"** (since the entire amount is personal/non-reimbursable).
3. Fill out the required fields, then **build and submit your Expense Report**.

**What happens next:** By submitting it as a personal expense, you authorize Amazon to **deduct the amount from future reimbursements** or from your account in Amazon Touchstone.

⚠️ **Note:** Repeated personal use of your corporate card may result in **card suspension**, so be careful going forward.

For step-by-step guidance, refer to the [How to Submit Expenses user guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) or visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: who do I talk to if I need help with an expense report
rows.answer:
You have several options for help with an expense report:

1. **Self-service first**: Visit the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) to resolve common issues or be directed to the right team.
2. **Expense Tool guidance**: Search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for how-to guides and video demos.
3. **Peer support**: Join the unofficial **#expenses-at-amazon-interest** Slack channel for advice from fellow Amazonians.
4. **Direct assistance**: Contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the [Employee Expenses & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: can I upgrade my fare using points or miles instead of paying
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can upgrade using personal loyalty miles**, but with some important limitations:

- After booking through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/), you must **contact the airline directly** to request a mileage upgrade — this cannot be done through the booking tool itself.

**Key limitations to be aware of:**
- Not all airlines allow mileage upgrades on tickets booked through third-party platforms
- Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are **typically ineligible**
- Review your specific fare rules before requesting an upgrade

For more details, refer to **section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: why do I have to book through the official travel program at all
rows.answer:
Booking through Amazon Travel & Events is **required** for several key reasons:

1. **Safety & Security** – 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details, plus automatic registration for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.

2. **Cost Savings** – Access to exclusive corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are **not available** on consumer booking sites.

3. **Booking Simplicity** – Access to pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.

4. **Sustainability** – Supports Amazon's Climate Pledge commitment by offering lower-emission travel options like rail and eco-conscious hotels.

5. **Service Quality** – Pre-screened providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

These benefits are simply not available through consumer booking sites.

rows.query: where is the contact info for travel support in my region
rows.answer:
You can find Travel Counselor contact information — including **phone, email, chat, and hours by country** — on the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki**:
👉 https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

**A few tips before you reach out:**
- Call from the phone number in your **Travel Profile** for proper routing
- Have your **Trip ID** ready for faster service
- If departing within **72 hours**, you're automatically prioritized in the call queue
- **Chat** is also available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (note: chat does not support new reservations)
- If you're outside your home country without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead

rows.query: what do I do if my flight gets cancelled by the airline
rows.answer:
Based on the available context, I don't have specific information about the process when an **airline cancels your flight**.

However, based on the general guidance provided, your best next steps would be to:

1. **Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to rebook your trip, as it supports modifications for most bookings.
2. **Contact a Travel Counselor** if self-service options are not available — they are available **24/7, 365 days a year**. Find contact details at the Amazon Travel & Events Wiki (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).

> **Tip:** If you're departing within 72 hours, you are automatically prioritized in the call queue. Have your **Trip ID ready** for faster service.

For policy details, refer to **section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations** (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: are baggage fees and seat selection reimbursable when I travel
rows.answer:
**Yes, with conditions:**

- **Baggage fees and seat selection costs are reimbursable** when you book the **Lowest Logical Fare** that includes these items.

- If you're traveling in **EMEA or APAC** and a fare that includes a standard seat or baggage exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, you should:
  1. Purchase the seat or baggage **separately as an ancillary item** directly with the airline
  2. **Expense the additional cost separately**
  3. **Save all receipts** for these ancillary purchases

- However, **upgrade fees and premium seating fees are NOT reimbursable** — you would pay that difference out of pocket.

For expensing, use the **"Airfare Fees"** expense type (GL Code 66012) for charges incurred during approved air travel. Review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy Section 4.7](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) for the full list of reimbursable and non-reimbursable airfare fees.

rows.query: how do I book a flight and hotel for a business trip
rows.answer:
## Booking a Flight and Hotel for Business Travel

**Step 1: Set Up Your Travel Profile**
Before your first booking, create a Travel Profile at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). You'll need to store your personal info, passport details (for international travel), and a credit card.

**Step 2: Book Through the Self-Service Booking Tool**
Log in to [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights and hotels directly through the tool.

**For Hotels, keep in mind:**
- Book the **lowest refundable standard room rate** on a single-occupancy basis
- Choose a hotel close to your work destination
- Amazon preferred hotels are recommended for competitive rates and flexible cancellation

**Last-Minute Bookings:**
- If your departure is **within 6 hours**, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours instead

**New to Amazon?**
Review the [New Hire Travel Playbook](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/newhire_playbook/) for a complete onboarding guide.

For full policy details, refer to the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication).

rows.query: can I fly business class and how much will the company pay back
rows.answer:
**You cannot expense business class — Amazon only reimburses the Lowest Logical Fare in economy/coach class.**

If you choose to upgrade to business class, here's how reimbursement works:

- **You pay the full ticket cost upfront** using your own payment method.
- **Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare** (economy portion).
- **You cover the difference** between the economy fare and the upgraded class out of pocket.
- **Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are not reimbursable.**

Note: Your manager will receive an email notification if you add a personal upgrade, but no action is required from them.

For full details, refer to **section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: what happens if my booking comes up as out of policy
rows.answer:
## Out of Policy Bookings

An **"Out of Policy"** flag means your selection exceeds policy guidelines, but **it does not prevent you from completing the booking**.

### Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation
- **Out of Policy**: Your selection exceeds guidelines but can be justified for business reasons — you can proceed by **providing a reason** when prompted.
- **Policy Violation**: Your selection breaks a hard rule (e.g., booking first class) — the booking **cannot be completed**.

### When You Can Proceed
You may book out of policy when there's a valid business reason, such as:
- A hotel closer to your business meetings
- An SUV during bad weather for safety reasons

Simply provide a brief reason when prompted. This acts as an audit tool to balance flexibility with safety and frugality.

rows.query: where do I find a trip I booked a few weeks ago
rows.answer:
You can find your previously booked trips by logging into the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and selecting **Trips** (or **Trips Library**, depending on your region) from the main menu — this gives you access to both current and past trips, including invoices.

If the trip is no longer visible or has been archived, open a request through **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records.

rows.query: what size rental car am I allowed to rent for work
rows.answer:
You may book **up to intermediate size vehicles** (mini, economy, or compact) through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Exceptions allowing larger vehicles include:**
- **Electric/hybrid vehicles** – You may book standard or full-size if that's the only electric/hybrid option available
- **3 or more travelers** sharing the vehicle
- **Large work equipment** requiring extra cargo space
- **Pre-approved medical reasons**
- **Safety reasons** (e.g., AWD/4WD in winter/blizzard-prone areas)

For full details, refer to [Section 6.1 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#6).

rows.query: I need to move my flight to a different day, how do I change it
rows.answer:
## How to Modify Your Flight

You can change your flight using the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Steps to modify your flight:**
1. Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Select your segment and choose **Modify**
4. Choose the flight you want to change
5. Update your details and search for new flights
6. Complete checkout

If self-service changes aren't available for your booking, contact a **Travel Counselor** at [TravelAndEvents/Support](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

You can also watch this quick video for guidance: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156).

rows.query: what kinds of flights am I actually allowed to book
rows.answer:
You must book the **Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class** with a seat assignment through the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Key points:
- **Non-stop flights** are preferred over connecting flights when possible and practical
- If a non-stop flight is **not** the Lowest Logical Fare, consult your manager to balance frugality and well-being
- **Upgrades** are allowed but you must pay the difference out of pocket — Amazon only reimburses the policy-approved economy fare

rows.query: how do I pick or change my seat on a flight
rows.answer:
## Selecting or Changing Your Seat

**During Booking:**
- Seat maps are available during the shopping path — use them to compare seat availability before selecting.
- A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout, but you can choose a different seat by viewing the seat map before completing your booking.

**After Booking:**
Once ticketed, you can change your seat by:
- Navigating to **Trips > My Trips** in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Using the **airline mobile app** directly (varies by airline)

**EMEA/APAC Travelers:**
If a fare including a seat exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, purchase the seat separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense it separately, and save your receipts.

For a visual walkthrough, watch: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

rows.query: I have a medical condition, how do I request a travel accommodation
rows.answer:
Contact **Disability and Leave Services (DLS)** to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment. Key points to note:

- **Only DLS can authorize** travel accommodations — your manager and Amazon Travel & Events cannot.
- Visit the [DLS site](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) for process details and requirements.
- If you are a **candidate or new hire**, contact the **Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)** instead.

You can also refer to **section 3.5 Travelers with Additional Needs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for further guidance.

rows.query: how do I set up my travel profile for the first time
rows.answer:
## Setting Up Your Travel Profile

To set up your Travel Profile for the first time:

1. **Go to the Self-Service Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
2. **Create your profile**, which will securely store:
   - Personal information (mobile phone number, date of birth)
   - Passport details (for international travel)
   - Corporate or personal credit card (required for hotel reservations)
   - Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

> ⚠️ **Important:** Some information will be pre-populated — verify that your **name exactly matches your government-issued ID**.

---

### Quick Start Guides
The guide you should use depends on your location:
- **Global/Regional:** [Traveler Guide](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FGlobal%20Public%2FAmazon%20Quick%20Start%20Guide%202025%2DFINAL%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FGlobal%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **India, China, Japan** — region-specific guides are also available.

For full policy details, refer to **section 3.4 Traveler Profiles** in the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec).

rows.query: do I get to keep my frequent flyer miles when I travel for work
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can keep any personal loyalty miles and points earned during business travel.

Here are the key things to know:

- Earn miles across **airlines, hotels, and rental cars** when booking through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Miles are **automatically credited** to your personal account after each trip
- Make sure your **loyalty numbers are added to your Travel Profile** and that your name matches exactly between your Travel Profile and loyalty program

**Note:** Employees based in **Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel** are not eligible due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

For more details, refer to **section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: I got into a car accident in a rental during a work trip, what now
rows.answer:
Here's what you need to do:

1. **Contact emergency services first** if there are injuries or immediate safety concerns.

2. **Report the damage** by visiting [Amazon Travel Risk Management](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) for the damage reporting process.

3. **Inform your manager** once the above steps are completed.

For coverage, note that Amazon provides **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)** insurance for business rentals booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) using preferred suppliers. Deductibles may apply depending on rental location.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.8 Car Accidents** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need a visa to travel internationally for business
rows.answer:
Yes, you may need a visa depending on your destination, citizenship, and trip details. Before traveling internationally, you must complete a **pre-travel immigration assessment** through the **International Business Travel Guided Experience**. This assessment will determine your specific visa and immigration requirements.

For more details, refer to [section 3.9 Visas and Passports](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: can I pay extra myself to upgrade on a really long flight
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can pay out of pocket to upgrade on a long flight.**

Amazon only reimburses the **Lowest Logical Fare in economy class**. You pay the difference between that and your upgraded cabin class yourself.

**How it works (Self-Service Booking Tool - desktop):**
1. Select your preferred upgraded cabin in the booking tool
2. On the Out of Policy pop-up, select **Skip suggestions**
3. Review the breakdown of the policy-approved amount vs. your personal cost at checkout
4. Confirm your **personal card** is the form of payment
5. Acknowledge the personal upgrade and book
6. Submit only the policy-approved portion for reimbursement

> **Note:** If you don't have access to the Self-Service Booking Tool, contact a Travel Counselor to arrange the upgrade instead.

**Also worth knowing:** If your flight is **14 hours or more**, you may qualify for **Premium Economy** under the PEA program (no out-of-pocket cost), if you've completed 4+ qualifying long-haul trips in the past 12 months. You can check your PEA status via [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91).

rows.query: is it ok to add a few personal vacation days onto a work trip
rows.answer:
Yes, you can extend a business trip to add personal vacation days, but you must **obtain your line manager's approval before extending the trip**.

Keep in mind:
- **Amazon only reimburses the business-related portion** of your trip.
- **All personal expenses during the extension are your responsibility.**

For more details, refer to section 3.6 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need my manager to approve my travel before I book
rows.answer:
**No.** Manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Your manager will automatically receive a copy of your itinerary once your booking is complete, but this is for **awareness only** and requires no action from them.

rows.query: do I need to buy insurance for a rental car on a business trip
rows.answer:
**No, you do not need to purchase additional rental car insurance for business travel.**

Amazon provides the following coverage when you book through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) using preferred suppliers:
- **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)**
- **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)**

**At the rental counter:** Decline any additional insurance offered, unless it is legally required and not already included in your rate.

Note: Deductibles may apply depending on the rental location.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: does the company provide travel insurance when I fly
rows.answer:
Yes, Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for business travel. You do **not** need to purchase separate travel insurance.

For full details on coverage and how to file claims, visit the [Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program](https://inside.hr.amazon.dev/content/inside/us/en/employment/your-career-global/insurance-and-assistance-program.html) page, or refer to **Section 10.4** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: the booking tool is broken, who do I contact for tech help
rows.answer: Go to **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to get technical support for the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool. You can use it to resolve login problems, system errors, payment display issues, or anything else preventing you from completing a booking.

rows.query: how do I reach a travel counselor or agent
rows.answer:
## Reaching a Travel Counselor

You can contact a Travel Counselor through the following channels:

- **Phone, Email, or Chat**: Find contact details (by country) on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/)
- **Chat**: Available directly within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) *(note: chat does not support new reservations)*

**Tips for faster service:**
- Call from the phone number listed in your **Travel Profile** to ensure proper routing
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- If departing within **72 hours**, you're automatically moved to the top of the call queue
- Don't want to wait on hold? Use the **Callback Feature**

**Traveling internationally?** If you don't have an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead of calling.

Travel Counselors are available **24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries.

rows.query: can I book a personal vacation through the work travel tool
rows.answer:
**No.** The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) is for **business travel only**. Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business use and are not available for personal trips.

For personal travel, visit **Amazon Extras** (extrasforamazon.com) for leisure travel promotions and personal booking options.

rows.query: what hotels am I allowed to book and what is the nightly limit
rows.answer:
## Hotel Booking Policy

You are allowed to book the **lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis**, close to your work destination. Bookings must be made through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or via a Travel Counselor, within Amazon Travel & Events.

**Preferred hotels** are recommended as they offer competitive rates, flexible cancellation policies, loyalty program eligibility, and safety/security vetting.

**For stays of 7+ nights**, Corporate Serviced Apartments are available (studio or one-bedroom, whichever is less expensive), but require **L8+ approval**.

> ⚠️ **Regarding a nightly rate limit:** The provided policy context does not include a specific nightly dollar limit. For that information, please refer to the [Global Travel and Expense Policy Section 5.0](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) or contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).

rows.query: why is the Amazon payment option not showing when I book a flight
rows.answer:
The Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution) is unavailable when your booking includes one or more charges **not eligible for central billing**. Common reasons include:

- **Personal upgrades** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Seat selection fees**
- **Baggage fees**
- **Other ancillary charges**
- **Personal travel segments**

Additionally, some airlines require **multi-factor authentication** for payment processing, which also requires an alternate form of payment.

For any of these situations, you'll need to use an **alternate payment method**.

If none of these conditions apply and you believe you should have access, contact **Amazon Travel Support** at https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/ for assistance.

rows.query: I got a hotel confirmation email I never asked for, why
rows.answer:
This is expected behavior from Amazon Travel & Events. The automated auditing system monitors your hotel reservations after booking and **automatically rebooks your hotel when a lower rate becomes available**.

Here's what you need to know:

- **Nothing changes** except your confirmation number and your rate (which is now lower)
- Your hotel property, check-in/check-out dates, and room type all stay the same
- **No action is required** from you

Just make sure to **use your new confirmation number** when checking in or contacting the hotel.

rows.query: I was charged for a hotel even though I cancelled it
rows.answer:
If you cancelled your hotel through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) and were still charged, contact **Amazon Travel Support** to investigate.

**Before contacting support, gather the following:**
- Confirmation that cancellation was within the hotel's cancellation period
- Date and time of your cancellation
- Receipt from the hotel for the no-show charge

You can reach Amazon Travel Support at: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

rows.query: how do I cancel or change a hotel reservation
rows.answer:
## Cancelling or Modifying a Hotel Reservation

Use the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

### Before you start, confirm all of the following:
- You have **not** already checked in
- Your request is **within the cancellation policy** guidelines
- The hotel has **not** been paid for in advance

If any of these conditions aren't met, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

---

### To Modify:
1. Log in to the Self-Service Booking Tool
2. Go to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Find your hotel reservation and select **Modify** (or **Cancel**)
4. Update check-in/check-out dates or select a different room type/rate
5. Review any price differences on the Checkout page
6. Select **Book Hotel** to confirm

> **Tip (Regional users):** If booking new dates or a different hotel, **book the new reservation first**, then cancel the old one — this prevents losing your reservation if the preferred hotel sells out.

---

For policy details, refer to [Section 3.10 – Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10).

rows.query: which expenses can I actually get reimbursed for
rows.answer:
Based on the context, reimbursable expenses are those that are:

- **Approved and necessary to the performance of your job**
- **Reflect good judgment and Amazon's Frugality Leadership Principle**

Common reimbursable expense categories include:

- **Travel:** Airfare, hotel, car rental, train, local transportation, personal mileage
- **Meals:** Individual travel meals, group business meals, per diems (in applicable countries)
- **Communications:** Mobile phone (up to $50/month for base plan), incremental mobile/roaming fees (up to $150/month)
- **Office/Equipment:** Office supplies, IT equipment (below CAPEX threshold), software
- **Other Travel Incidentals:** Laundry, tips/gratuities, passport/visa fees, currency exchange fees, parking

**Important caveats:**
- You should **consult the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication)** before purchasing to confirm eligibility
- **Manager or PXT approval does not override policy** — if it's not covered by policy, it's not reimbursable
- Some expense types are **region-specific or role-restricted**
- Switzerland-based employees must also consult the [Switzerland T&E Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/703733/publication)

For a full list, review the Global Travel and Expense Policy or visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: what is the deadline to submit my expenses after a trip
rows.answer: You must submit your expense report within **60 days of the transaction date**. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses and corporate card transactions. Missing this deadline for corporate card transactions may result in your card being suspended.

rows.query: how do I file an expense report
rows.answer:
## How to Submit an Expense Report

Amazon is currently migrating from **Concur** to **Emburse**, so the tool you use depends on your location.

**Step-by-step guides are available here:**
- **Concur:** [Concur User Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide)
- **Emburse:** [How to Submit Expenses](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)

Visit the **Expense Tool Migration Hub** to check the migration timeline for your location.

For additional help, you can also search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for video demos and how-to guides.

rows.query: my expense report got rejected, what do I do now
rows.answer:
## Your Expense Report Was Rejected

Here's what to do:

1. **Check the Expense Tool** at [expenses.corp.amazon.com](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) — your rejected report will be returned there with **guidance on how to fix it**. You may need to:
   - Submit legible receipts
   - Provide additional justification
   - Select a different expense type

2. **If you think it was rejected in error** — review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication), then add a detailed justification in the **comment field** and resubmit.

3. **If additional approvals are needed** — your manager or skip-level may be added as a reviewer. No action is needed from you unless you want to follow up with them directly.

> ⚠️ **Note:** Resubmitting does **not** restart the 60-day submission deadline clock.

**Need further help?** Contact the Amazon Global Expense Team via the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: I can't figure out which expense category to use
rows.answer:
To find the right expense type, refer to the **Expense Type Guide**:

- **Concur users**: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/
- **Emburse users**: https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide

This guide contains detailed descriptions of every available expense type, including country-specific restrictions.

If you don't see the expense type you need in the Expense Tool, it likely isn't available based on your location.

rows.query: are meals paid for while I'm traveling for business
rows.answer:
Yes, meals are covered during business travel. Amazon reimburses meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business.

**Key requirements:**
- **Keep itemized receipts** showing individual meal items to submit with your expense report
- **Group meals:** The most senior person should pay and list all attendees when submitting. Only split the bill if each person is expensing their own meal separately
- **Meals with government officials** require additional review — check the Gift Reporting Guidelines and consult your manager before expensing

For specific per diem amounts and regional expense limits, visit the [Travel Reimbursement Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/).

rows.query: can I expense my cell phone bill
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can expense your cell phone bill** (subject to eligibility).

**Who's eligible:** Employees L4–L8. Exceptions apply for L1–L3 and L10+.

**How to submit:**
1. Go to the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/)
2. Select **"Mobile Phone"** as the Expense Type
3. Enter your total base plan cost
4. Select your business use percentage:
   - 25% – infrequent use
   - 50% – mixed use
   - 75% – significant use
   - 100% – primarily work use
5. Attach your **full itemized bill** showing your name and/or phone number

**Reimbursement cap:** Up to **$50 USD/month** on your base plan. The system calculates this automatically.

**Extra charges (roaming, hotspots, etc.):** Use the **"Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees"** Expense Type for up to **$150/month** in work-related incremental costs like international roaming or mobile hotspots.

> Note: The base plan reimbursement does **not** cover additional lines, wearable device lines, or home internet.

Review [Section 12.6 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting to confirm your eligibility.

rows.query: how long until I actually get my reimbursement money
rows.answer:
Most expense reports are processed and paid **within 4 business days**. However, it may take longer if your report:

- **Is flagged for additional review** (likely if it's been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days)
- **Requires additional approvals** from your manager or skip-level manager

To avoid delays, review the [Expense Report Checklist](https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) before submitting to ensure your report is complete, compliant, and accurate.

rows.query: I lost the receipt for something, can I still expense it
rows.answer:
Yes, you have three options if you're missing a receipt:

1. **Contact the merchant for a new receipt** – This is the preferred option and the **only acceptable solution** for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals.

2. **Submit a bank/card statement** – If you can't get a merchant receipt, submit a statement showing your name, transaction amount, and date (redact personal details and unrelated charges). This requires **manager approval**.

3. **Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD)** – A last resort for **low-value expenses only**. Not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete it yourself (delegates cannot) by checking "Missing Receipt" in the Expense Tool. Also requires **manager approval**, and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access.

rows.query: I accidentally paid for a personal thing on my corporate card
rows.answer:
**No worries — here's what to do:**

1. Find the charge in the **Expense Tool** under your eWallet.
2. Select the Expense Type **"Personal Expense"** (if the entire amount is personal).
3. Fill out the required fields, then submit the expense report.

**If only part of the transaction was personal** (e.g., a mixed purchase):
- Apply the appropriate expense type for the business portion.
- Under **Allocation**, click **Add Allocation** and select **"PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense"**, then adjust the amounts accordingly.

**What happens next:**
By submitting as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or your account in **Amazon Touchstone**.

> ⚠️ Note: Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in **card suspension**, so be sure to resolve this promptly.

For step-by-step guidance, refer to the [How to Submit Expenses user guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) or visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: who do I talk to if I need help with an expense report
rows.answer:
You have several options for help with an expense report:

- **Self-service:** Visit the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER), where you can find solutions to common issues or be guided to submit a ticket to the right team.
- **Expense Tool guidance:** Search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for video demos and how-to guides.
- **Community support:** Join the unofficial [#expenses-at-amazon-interest](https://amazon.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C08BNHGCAJ1) Slack channel for advice from fellow Amazonians.
- **Direct assistance:** Contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the [Employee Expenses & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: can I upgrade my fare using points or miles instead of paying
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can upgrade your fare using personal loyalty miles. Here's what you need to know:

- **Book your travel first** through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/), then **contact the airline directly** to request the mileage upgrade — it cannot be processed through the booking tool itself.

**Important limitations to be aware of:**
- Not all airlines allow mileage upgrades on tickets booked through third-party platforms
- Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically **not eligible**
- Review your specific fare rules before requesting an upgrade

For more details, refer to **section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: why do I have to book through the official travel program at all
rows.answer:
Booking through Amazon Travel & Events is **required** for several key reasons:

1. **Safety & Security** – 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details, plus automatic registration for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.

2. **Cost Savings** – Access to exclusive corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are **not available** on consumer booking sites.

3. **Booking Simplicity** – Access to pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.

4. **Sustainability** – Supports Amazon's Climate Pledge commitment by offering lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotel options.

5. **Service Quality** – Pre-screened travel providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

These benefits are simply not available through consumer booking sites, which is why external booking methods are strictly prohibited except when no availability exists through the official tool.

rows.query: where is the contact info for travel support in my region
rows.answer:
You can find Travel Counselor contact information — including **phone, email, chat, and hours by country** — on the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki**:
👉 https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

**Tips before contacting:**
- Call from the phone number in your **Travel Profile** for proper routing
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- If departing within **72 hours**, you'll be automatically prioritized
- **Chat** is available within the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (booktravel.corp.amazon.com), though it does not support new reservations
- If calling from outside your home country without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead

rows.query: what do I do if my flight gets cancelled by the airline
rows.answer:
Based on the available context, I don't have specific information about what to do when an airline cancels your flight.

However, the context does suggest that if standard self-service options aren't available for your booking, you can:

- **Contact a Travel Counselor** for assistance: [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/)
- Use the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to rebook your trip if applicable.

For more detailed guidance, I'd recommend reaching out to Amazon Travel Support directly.

rows.query: are baggage fees and seat selection reimbursable when I travel
rows.answer:
**It depends on how you book:**

- **If included in your fare:** Book the Lowest Logical Fare with seat assignment and baggage included — these are covered as part of the fare.

- **For EMEA and APAC travelers:** If fares including a standard seat or baggage exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits, you should purchase these separately as ancillary items directly with the airline, expense the additional cost separately, and save all receipts.

**What is NOT reimbursable:**
- **Upgrade fees and premium seating fees** are explicitly not reimbursable.
- Personal upgrade seat selection fees are considered personal charges and must be paid out of pocket.

For full details, refer to **section 4.7 Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: how do I book a flight and hotel for a business trip
rows.answer:
## Booking a Flight and Hotel for a Business Trip

Here's how to get started:

1. **Set up your Travel Profile** (if you haven't already) at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) before your first booking. Make sure your name exactly matches your government-issued ID.

2. **Book your travel** by logging into the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and searching for flights and hotels.

> **Note:** You'll need a corporate or personal credit card on file in your profile to secure hotel reservations.

### Special Cases:
- **Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure):** Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours.
- **New to Amazon?** Review the [New Hire Travel Playbook](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/newhire_playbook/) for a complete onboarding guide.
- **India employees:** If your full name exceeds 58 characters, you must book through a Travel Counselor instead of online.

For policy details, refer to section 3.3 of the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#66c58a98-3506-6b1f-2814-abf4f9de7f38).

rows.query: can I fly business class and how much will the company pay back
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can fly business class**, but Amazon will only reimburse the **Lowest Logical Fare in economy class**. You must pay the difference between that amount and the business class fare **out of pocket**.

**How reimbursement works:**
- You pay the **full ticket cost upfront**
- You submit only the **policy-approved economy fare** for reimbursement
- The upgrade cost difference is **your personal expense**

The booking process varies depending on your region:
- **Global Access users:** Book directly in the Self-Service Booking Tool — it will display both the policy-approved amount and your personal upgrade cost at checkout.
- **Regional users:** Contact a Travel Counselor and obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and the upgraded class.

For full details, refer to **section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: what happens if my booking comes up as out of policy
rows.answer:
## Out of Policy Bookings

An **"Out of Policy"** flag identifies options that don't comply with the Global Travel and Expense Policy, but **it does not prevent you from completing your booking**.

### Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation
- **Out of Policy**: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but *can* be justified for business reasons — you'll need to **provide a reason** for your selection.
- **Policy Violation**: Your selection breaks a hard rule (e.g., booking first class) — the booking **cannot be completed**.

### When You Can Proceed
You may book out of policy when there's a valid business reason, such as:
- A hotel that's closer to your meetings
- An SUV during bad weather for safety reasons

Simply **provide a brief reason when prompted**. This is used as an audit tool to balance flexibility with policy compliance.

rows.query: where do I find a trip I booked a few weeks ago
rows.answer:
Go to the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and select **Trips** (or **Trips Library**, depending on your region) from the main menu — this shows both current and past trips, including invoices.

If the trip is no longer visible or has been archived, submit a request through **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records.

rows.query: what size rental car am I allowed to rent for work
rows.answer:
You are allowed to book **up to intermediate size vehicles** (mini, economy, or compact) through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool.

**Exceptions allowing larger vehicles include:**
- **Electric/hybrid vehicles** — if only standard or full-size options are available
- **3 or more travelers** sharing the vehicle
- **Large work equipment** requiring extra cargo space
- **Pre-approved medical reasons**
- **Safety reasons** (e.g., AWD/4WD in winter/blizzard-prone areas)

rows.query: I need to move my flight to a different day, how do I change it
rows.answer:
## How to Modify Your Flight

You can change your flight using the **[Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)**. Here's how:

1. Log in to the tool and navigate to **Trips > Upcoming**
2. Select your trip
3. Select your segment and click **Modify**
4. Choose the flight you want to change
5. Update your details and search for new flights
6. Complete checkout to confirm

You can also watch this quick video for guidance: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156)

> **Note:** If online self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

rows.query: what kinds of flights am I actually allowed to book
rows.answer:
You must book the **Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class** with a seat assignment through the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Key points:
- **Non-stop flights** are allowed even if not the cheapest option, but if a non-stop isn't the Lowest Logical Fare, check with your manager first.
- **Premium Economy** is only available if you qualify for the PEA program (4+ one-way flights over 14 hours in the last 12 months), and only applies to flights **14 hours or longer**.

You should also prioritize **preferred suppliers** listed in the booking tool for added benefits and flexibility.

rows.query: how do I pick or change my seat on a flight
rows.answer:
## Selecting or Changing Your Seat

**During booking:**
- Seat maps are available during the shopping path to compare availability across flights.
- A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout, but you can view the seat map and choose a different seat before completing your booking.

**After booking:**
Once ticketed, you can change your seat by:
- Navigating to **Trips > My Trips** in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Using the **airline mobile app** directly (varies by airline)

**Note for EMEA/APAC travelers:** If a fare including a seat exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, purchase the seat separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense it separately, and save your receipt.

For a visual walkthrough, watch the quick video: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

rows.query: I have a medical condition, how do I request a travel accommodation
rows.answer:
Contact **Disability and Leave Services (DLS)** to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment or limitation.

**Important notes:**
- **Only DLS can authorize** travel accommodations — Amazon Travel & Events and your manager cannot.
- Visit the [DLS site](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) for process details and requirements.
- If you are a **candidate or new hire**, contact the **Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)** instead.

You can also refer to **section 3.5 Travelers with Additional Needs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for further guidance.

rows.query: how do I set up my travel profile for the first time
rows.answer:
## Setting Up Your Travel Profile

To set up your Travel Profile for the first time:

1. **Go to the Self-Service Booking Tool** at https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/
2. **Create your profile**, which will store:
   - Personal information (mobile phone number and date of birth)
   - Passport details (for international travel)
   - Corporate or personal credit card (required for hotel reservations)
   - Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

> ⚠️ **Important:** Some information will be pre-populated — verify that your **name exactly matches your government-issued ID**.

---

### Quick Start Guides (by region):
- **Global:** [Traveler Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/NewTravelSystem/)
- **India:** [Traveler Guide](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FProfile%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **China:** [Traveler Guide](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripSouce%20China%5FBCD%20Quick%20Start%20Guide%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **Japan:** [Quick Start Videos](https://broadcast.amazon.com/channels/1303/playlists/18

rows.query: do I get to keep my frequent flyer miles when I travel for work
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can keep any personal loyalty miles and points earned when traveling for business.

Here are the key points to know:

- This applies to **airlines, hotels, and rental cars** booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).
- Add your loyalty program numbers to your **Travel Profile** to ensure miles are automatically credited after each trip.
- Make sure your **Travel Profile name matches your loyalty program name exactly** to avoid missing credits.
- If miles aren't credited after your trip, contact the travel provider directly.

**Note:** Employees based in **Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel** are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

rows.query: I got into a car accident in a rental during a work trip, what now
rows.answer:
Here's what you need to do:

1. **Contact emergency services first** if there are any injuries or immediate safety concerns.
2. **Visit Amazon Travel Risk Management** (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) to complete the damage reporting process.
3. **Inform your manager** once the above steps are completed.

For insurance coverage details, since you were in a rental, Amazon provides **CDW and LDW insurance** when the rental was booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) using preferred suppliers. Note that deductibles may apply depending on your rental location.

For full details, refer to:
- **Section 6.8 Car Accidents** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy
- **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy

rows.query: do I need a visa to travel internationally for business
rows.answer:
You must complete a **pre-travel immigration assessment** through the **International Business Travel Guided Experience** before traveling internationally. This assessment will determine your specific visa and immigration requirements based on:

- Your **destination country**
- Your **citizenship**
- Your **trip details** (duration and purpose)

For full details, refer to [section 3.9 Visas and Passports](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: can I pay extra myself to upgrade on a really long flight
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — there are two options depending on your situation:

**Option 1: PEA Program (Free upgrade eligibility)**
If you've completed **4+ one-way trips of 14 hours or more** in the last 12 months (booked through Amazon Travel), you may automatically qualify for **Premium Economy** at no personal cost. No action needed — eligibility is monitored daily. You can check your status via [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91).

**Option 2: Pay the difference yourself**
If you don't qualify for PEA, you can still upgrade by paying the difference between the **Lowest Logical Fare (economy)** and the upgraded class **out of pocket**. Amazon will only reimburse the policy-approved economy fare. Note:
- Upgrade and premium seating fees are **not reimbursable**
- Your manager will receive an email notification (no action required)
- The Amazon form of payment **won't be available** for the upgrade portion — you'll need a personal payment method

For booking instructions, refer to the [Global Travel and Expense Policy, section 4.1](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1).

rows.query: is it ok to add a few personal vacation days onto a work trip
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can extend a business trip to add personal time, but you need to meet a couple of requirements:

1. **Get line manager approval** before extending your trip.
2. **Amazon only reimburses the business portion** of your travel costs. Any personal expenses during the extension are your responsibility.

For more details, refer to **section 3.6 Extended Trip for Personal Time** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need my manager to approve my travel before I book
rows.answer:
**No**, — manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Your manager will automatically receive a copy of your itinerary after you complete a booking, but this is **for awareness only** and requires no action from them.

rows.query: do I need to buy insurance for a rental car on a business trip
rows.answer:
**No, you do not need to purchase additional rental car insurance for business trips.**

Amazon provides **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)** and **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)** coverage automatically when you:
- Book through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (booktravel.corp.amazon.com)
- Use preferred suppliers

**At the rental counter:** Decline any additional insurance offered, unless it is legally required and not already included in your rate.

Note that deductibles may apply depending on your rental location. For full details, refer to **section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: does the company provide travel insurance when I fly
rows.answer:
Yes. Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for business travel, so you do **not** need to purchase separate travel insurance.

For details on coverage and how to file claims, visit the **Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program** page, or refer to **section 10.4** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: the booking tool is broken, who do I contact for tech help
rows.answer: Go to **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to get technical support for the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool. You can use it to resolve login problems, system errors, payment display issues, or anything else preventing you from completing a booking.

rows.query: how do I reach a travel counselor or agent
rows.answer:
## Reaching a Travel Counselor

Here are the ways to contact a Travel Counselor:

- **Phone:** Call the number listed in your **Travel Profile** (using your profile number ensures proper call routing and identification)
- **Chat:** Available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries *(note: chat does not support new reservations)*
- **Email:** Also available as a contact option
- Find full contact details (phone, email, chat, and hours by country) on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/)

**Travel Counselors are available 24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries.

### Tips for Faster Service:
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- Ensure your **Travel Profile** has your current phone number
- Departing **within 72 hours**? You are automatically moved to the top of the call queue
- Don't want to wait on hold? Use the **Callback Feature**

### If Traveling Internationally:
If you don't have an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead of calling.

rows.query: can I book a personal vacation through the work travel tool
rows.answer:
**No.** The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) is for **business travel only**. Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business travel and cannot be used for personal trips.

For personal/leisure travel, visit **Amazon Extras** (extrasforamazon.com) instead.

rows.query: what hotels am I allowed to book and what is the nightly limit
rows.answer:
## Hotel Booking Policy

You are allowed to book the **lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis**, booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or a Travel Counselor. Amazon preferred hotels are recommended.

**Regarding a nightly rate limit:** I don't have that information in the available context. You may want to check the [Global Travel and Expense Policy Section 5.0](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) or contact your Travel Counselor for details on any nightly rate caps.

rows.query: why is the Amazon payment option not showing when I book a flight
rows.answer:
The Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution) is unavailable when your booking includes charges that aren't eligible for central billing. Common reasons include:

- **Personal upgrades** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Seat selection fees**
- **Baggage fees**
- **Other ancillary charges**
- **Personal travel segments**

Additionally, some airlines require **multi-factor authentication** for payment processing, which also requires an alternate payment method.

For any of these cases, you'll need to use an alternate payment method instead.

If none of these conditions apply and you believe you should have access, contact **Amazon Travel Support** at https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/.

rows.query: I got a hotel confirmation email I never asked for, why
rows.answer:
## Automatic Hotel Rebooking

You received a new confirmation because **Amazon Travel & Events' automated auditing system found a lower rate for your hotel and automatically rebooked it** to capture the savings.

**What stays the same:**
- Hotel property
- Check-in/check-out dates
- Room type and preferences

**What changes:**
- Your confirmation number (use the **new one** when checking in)
- Your rate (it's now lower)

**No action is needed from you** — the rebooking happens automatically. Just make sure to use your new confirmation number when checking in or contacting the hotel.

rows.query: I was charged for a hotel even though I cancelled it
rows.answer:
If you cancelled your hotel through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** and were still charged, contact **Amazon Travel Support** to investigate.

Before reaching out, make sure you have the following ready:

- Confirmation that your cancellation was **within the hotel's cancellation period**
- The **date and time** of your cancellation
- The **receipt** from the hotel for the no-show charge

You can reach Amazon Travel Support at: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

rows.query: how do I cancel or change a hotel reservation
rows.answer:
## Canceling or Modifying a Hotel Reservation
