You can make most changes directly through the **[Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)**.

### Before You Start
Make sure **all** of the following apply:
- You have **not** checked into the hotel
- Your request is within the **cancellation policy guidelines**
- The hotel has **not been paid in advance**

If any of these conditions aren't met, contact a **[Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/)** for assistance.

---

### How to Modify
1. Log in to the booking tool
2. Go to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Find your hotel reservation and select **Modify** (or **Cancel**)
4. Update check-in/check-out dates or room type
5. Review changes and price differences, then select **Book Hotel** to confirm

> **Tip:** If booking an entirely new hotel, **book the new one first**, then cancel the original — this prevents losing your reservation if your preferred hotel sells out.

---

For policy details, refer to **[Section 3.10 – Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10)**.

rows.query: which expenses can I actually get reimbursed for
rows.answer:
Based on the policy context, reimbursable expenses are those that are **approved, necessary to your job, and reflect good judgment and Amazon's Frugality principle**.

Here are the main categories of reimbursable expenses:

**Travel**
- Airfare, hotel, car rental (must be booked through Amazon's TMC)
- Train, local transportation (taxis, Uber, Lyft)
- Personal car mileage
- Parking during business travel

**Meals & Incidentals**
- Individual travel meals
- Group business meals
- Laundry, tips/gratuities
- Passport/visa fees for business travel
- Currency exchange fees

**Technology & Equipment**
- IT/office equipment below CAPEX threshold
- Software expenses
- Mobile phone (up to $50/month for base plan)
- Incremental travel mobile fees (up to $150/month)

**Other Business Expenses**
- Office supplies
- Training & professional development
- Professional dues/subscriptions
- Corporate shipping
- Marketing & advertising (where applicable)

**Important notes:**
- **Manager or PXT approval does NOT override policy** — always check the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before purchasing
- Some expense types are **region-specific or role-restricted**
- Non-Amazonians (contractors, candidates) follow a separate [Non-Employee Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/610963/publication)

For the full list, consult the Global Travel and Expense Policy directly.

rows.query: what is the deadline to submit my expenses after a trip
rows.answer: You must submit your expense reports within **60 days of the transaction date**. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses and corporate card transactions. Note that if you fail to submit corporate card transactions within this 60-day window, your corporate card may be suspended.

rows.query: how do I file an expense report
rows.answer:
## How to Submit an Expense Report

Amazon is currently migrating from **Concur** to **Emburse**, so the tool you use depends on your location.

**Step-by-step guides are available for both tools:**
- **Concur:** [How to Submit Expenses - Concur User Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide)
- **Emburse:** [How to Submit Expenses - Emburse User Guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)

Visit the **Expense Tool Migration Hub** to find out which tool applies to your location.

**Key reminders:**
- Submit expense reports **within 60 days** of the transaction date
- Most reports are processed and paid within **4 business days**

For additional help, visit the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for video demos and how-to guides.

rows.query: my expense report got rejected, what do I do now
rows.answer:
## Your Expense Report Was Rejected

Here's what you should do:

1. **Check the Expense Tool** (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) — Your rejected report will be returned there with guidance on how to fix and resubmit it. You may need to:
   - Submit legible receipts
   - Provide additional justification
   - Select a different expense type

2. **If you think it was rejected in error** — Review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication), then add a detailed justification in the **comment field** and resubmit.

3. **If additional approvals are needed** — Your manager or skip-level manager will be added as a reviewer. No action is required from you unless you want to follow up with them directly.

4. **Need help resubmitting?** — Contact the Amazon Global Expense Team via the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

> **Note:** If you need to resubmit, the 60-day submission deadline clock does **not** restart from the rejection date.

rows.query: I can't figure out which expense category to use
rows.answer:
You can refer to the **Expense Type Guide** for detailed descriptions of every available expense type, including country-specific restrictions:

- **Emburse users:** [Expense Type Guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide)
- **Concur users:** [Expense Type Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/)

If you don't see a specific expense type in the tool, it likely isn't available to you based on your location.

If you'd like, share the type of expense you're trying to categorize and I can help point you to the right expense type.

rows.query: are meals paid for while I'm traveling for business
rows.answer:
Yes, meals are covered during business travel. Amazon reimburses meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business.

**Key requirements:**
- Retain the **itemized receipt** showing individual meal items to submit with your expense report
- Use the **"Individual Travel Meal"** expense type when submitting
- For **group meals while traveling**, the most senior person should pay and list all attendees when submitting

**Additional notes:**
- Meals with government officials require additional steps — review the Gift Reporting Guidelines and consult your manager before expensing
- Specific per diem limits and regional guidance are available in the [Travel Reimbursement Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/)
- Amazon expects employees to demonstrate **Frugality** in spending decisions

rows.query: can I expense my cell phone bill
rows.answer:
**Yes, your cell phone bill is reimbursable** if you're an employee at levels **L4–L8**.

Here's how it works:

- **Reimbursement cap:** Up to **$50 USD/month** for your personal mobile phone base plan
- **Expense Type to use:** "Mobile Phone" in the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/)
- **What to enter:** Your total base plan cost + your business use percentage:
  - 25% – infrequent use
  - 50% – mixed use
  - 75% – significant use
  - 100% – primarily work use
- **Required documentation:** Attach your full itemized bill showing your name and/or phone number
- The system will automatically calculate your reimbursement, capped at $50

**Note:** Exceptions apply for L1–L3 and L10+. The base plan reimbursement does **not** cover additional lines, wearables, or home internet.

**Extra charges while traveling?** You can also expense up to **$150/month** for incremental work-related costs (e.g., international roaming, mobile hotspots) using the **"Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees"** expense type.

Review [Section 12.6 of the Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting to confirm your eligibility.

rows.query: how long until I actually get my reimbursement money
rows.answer:
Most expense reports are processed and paid within **4 business days**. However, it may take longer if your report:

- **Requires additional review** (reports pending with no movement for more than 3 business days have likely been flagged)
- **Requires additional approvals** from your manager or skip-level manager

To avoid delays, review the [Expense Report Checklist](https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) before submitting to ensure your report is complete, compliant, and accurate.

rows.query: I lost the receipt for something, can I still expense it
rows.answer:
Yes, you have three options if you're missing a receipt:

1. **Contact the merchant for a new receipt** – This is the preferred option and the **only acceptable solution** for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals.

2. **Submit a bank/card statement** – If you can't get a merchant receipt, submit a statement showing your name, transaction amount, and date (redact personal details). This requires **manager approval**.

3. **Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD)** – A last resort for **low-value expenses only**. Not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete it yourself (delegates cannot) by checking "Missing Receipt" in the Expense Tool. This also requires **manager approval**, and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access.

rows.query: I accidentally paid for a personal thing on my corporate card
rows.answer:
No worries, it happens! Here's what to do:

1. Find the corporate card charge in the **Expense Tool** under your eWallet.
2. Select the Expense Type **"Personal Expense"** (if the entire amount is personal).
3. Fill out the required fields, then submit your Expense Report.

**If only part of the transaction was personal:**
- Apply the appropriate expense type for the business portion.
- Under **Allocation**, click **Add Allocation** and select **"PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense"**.
- Adjust the amounts to reflect what is personal, then submit.

**What happens next:** By submitting as a personal expense, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or via your account in **Amazon Touchstone**.

⚠️ Note: Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in **card suspension**, so be careful going forward.

For additional help, visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: who do I talk to if I need help with an expense report
rows.answer:
You have several options for help with an expense report:

- **Self-service/Submit a ticket:** Visit the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) to solve common issues or be directed to the right team.
- **Expense tool guidance:** Search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for how-to guides and video demos.
- **Peer support:** Join the [#expenses-at-amazon-interest](https://amazon.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C08BNHGCAJ1) Slack channel for advice from fellow Amazonians.
- **Direct assistance:** Contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the [Employee Expenses & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: can I upgrade my fare using points or miles instead of paying
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can upgrade your fare using personal loyalty miles, with some important caveats:

- After booking through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/), **contact the airline directly** to request the mileage upgrade — this cannot be done through the booking tool itself.

**Key limitations to be aware of:**
- Not all airlines allow mileage upgrades for tickets booked through third-party platforms
- Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically **ineligible** for mileage upgrades
- Review your specific fare rules before requesting an upgrade

For more details, refer to **section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: why do I have to book through the official travel program at all
rows.answer:
Booking through Amazon Travel & Events is **required** for several key reasons:

1. **Safety & Security** – You get 24/7 emergency support, and your travel is automatically registered for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.

2. **Cost Savings** – Amazon negotiates exclusive corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rentals that are **not available** on consumer booking sites.

3. **Booking Simplicity** – Access to pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards, all in one place.

4. **Sustainability** – Lower-emission travel options (like rail and eco-conscious hotels) are available, aligned with Amazon's Climate Pledge commitment.

5. **Service Quality** – Pre-screened providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

Note: Using external booking methods is **strictly prohibited** except when no availability exists through the official tool.

rows.query: where is the contact info for travel support in my region
rows.answer:
You can find Travel Counselor contact information — including **phone, email, chat, and hours by country** — on the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki**:
👉 https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

**Quick tips:**
- Call from the phone number listed in your **Travel Profile** for proper routing
- **Chat** is available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (note: chat does not support new reservations)
- Travel Counselors are available **24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries
- If departing within **72 hours**, you're automatically prioritized in the call queue

rows.query: what do I do if my flight gets cancelled by the airline
rows.answer:
Based on the available context, I don't have specific information about the process for handling airline-initiated flight cancellations.

However, based on the related information available, I'd recommend:

1. **Contact a Travel Counselor** – They are available **24/7, 365 days a year** and can assist with booking issues. Find their contact details (phone, email, chat) on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).
2. **Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to check your trip and explore rebooking options.

For faster service when calling, have your **Trip ID ready** and ensure your Travel Profile has your current phone number.

rows.query: are baggage fees and seat selection reimbursable when I travel
rows.answer:
**Baggage fees and seat selection reimbursability depends on how they are purchased:**

- **When included in your fare:** Book the Lowest Logical Fare with seat assignment and baggage included — these are reimbursable as part of the fare.

- **EMEA and APAC travelers:** If fares including a standard seat or baggage exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits, you may purchase these separately as ancillary items directly with the airline and expense the additional cost. Save all receipts.

- **Upgrade-related seat/baggage fees:** Premium seating fees and upgrade fees are **not reimbursable**. Amazon only reimburses the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class.

For full details, refer to **section 4.7 Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: how do I book a flight and hotel for a business trip
rows.answer:
To book a flight and hotel for a business trip:

1. **Set up your Travel Profile first** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (required before booking). Make sure your name matches your government-issued ID.

2. **Book online** by logging into the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and searching for flights and hotels.

**Note:** You'll need a corporate or personal credit card on file to secure hotel reservations.

**For last-minute travel:**
- Within 6 hours of departure → Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours.
- Same-day travel outside the 6-hour window → Book online through the Self-Service Tool.

If you're a new hire, review the [New Hire Travel Playbook](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/newhire_playbook/) for a complete onboarding guide.

rows.query: can I fly business class and how much will the company pay back
rows.answer:
## Flying Business Class

**Yes, you can upgrade to business class**, but Amazon will only reimburse the **Lowest Logical Fare in economy class**. You must pay the difference between the economy fare and the business class fare **out of pocket**.

### How it works:
- You pay the **full upgraded fare upfront**
- Amazon reimburses only the **policy-approved economy portion**
- The personal upgrade cost is **your responsibility**

### How to book (Global Access employees):
1. Select your preferred cabin class in the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (desktop only)
2. The fare indicator will show the policy-approved amount vs. your personal upgrade cost
3. On the Out of Policy pop-up, select **"Skip suggestions"**
4. Confirm your **personal card** is set as the payment method
5. Acknowledge the personal upgrade and book

Your invoice will include a **Personal Upgrade Summary** to use when submitting your expense report for the reimbursable portion.

> **Note:** Your manager will receive an email notification about the upgrade, but no action is required from them.

For regional employees, contact a **Travel Counselor** to arrange the upgrade instead of using the self-service tool.

rows.query: what happens if my booking comes up as out of policy
rows.answer:
## Out of Policy Bookings

The **"Out of Policy"** flag identifies options that don't comply with the Global Travel and Expense Policy, but **it does not prevent you from completing your booking**.

### Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation
- **Out of Policy**: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but *can* be justified for business reasons — you'll be prompted to provide a reason.
- **Policy Violation**: Your selection breaks a hard rule (e.g., booking first class) — the booking **cannot** be completed.

### When You Can Proceed
You may book out of policy when there's a valid business reason, such as:
- A hotel closer to your business meetings
- An SUV during bad weather for safety reasons

Simply provide a brief explanation when prompted. This reason serves as an audit tool to balance flexibility with frugality.

rows.query: where do I find a trip I booked a few weeks ago
rows.answer:
You can find your previously booked trips by logging into the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and selecting **Trips** (or **Trips Library**, depending on your region) from the main menu. This gives you access to both current and past trips, including invoices.

If the trip is no longer visible or has been archived, submit a request through **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records.

rows.query: what size rental car am I allowed to rent for work
rows.answer:
You are allowed to book **up to intermediate size vehicles** (mini, economy, or compact) through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Exceptions allowing larger vehicles:**
- **Electric/hybrid vehicles** – If only standard or full-size electric/hybrid options are available, you may book those
- **3 or more travelers** sharing the same rental vehicle
- **Large work equipment** requiring additional cargo space
- **Pre-approved medical reasons**
- **Safety exceptions** (e.g., AWD/4WD in winter/blizzard-prone locations)

For full details, refer to [Section 6.1 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#6).

rows.query: I need to move my flight to a different day, how do I change it
rows.answer:
## Changing Your Flight to a Different Day

You can modify your flight using the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Steps to modify your flight:**
1. Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Select your segment and select **Modify**
4. Choose the flight to change
5. Update your details and search for new flights
6. Complete checkout

If self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a **Travel Counselor** for assistance.

You can also watch this quick video for guidance: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156)

rows.query: what kinds of flights am I actually allowed to book
rows.answer:
You must book the **Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class** that includes a seat assignment, using the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Non-stop flights:** You may prioritize non-stop over connecting flights when possible and practical. However, if the non-stop option is *not* the Lowest Logical Fare, consult your manager to find the right balance between frugality and well-being.

For full details, refer to section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: how do I pick or change my seat on a flight
rows.answer:
## Selecting or Changing Your Seat

**During booking:**
- Seat maps are available during the shopping path to compare availability across flights.
- A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout, but you can choose a different seat by viewing the seat map before completing your booking.

**After booking:**
You can change your seat once ticketed by:
- Navigating to **Trips > My Trips** in the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Using the **airline mobile app** directly (varies by airline)

**Note for EMEA and APAC travelers:** If a fare that includes a seat assignment exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, purchase the seat separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense it separately, and save your receipt.

For a visual walkthrough, watch the quick video: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

rows.query: I have a medical condition, how do I request a travel accommodation
rows.answer:
Contact **Disability and Leave Services (DLS)** to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment or limitation. Key points to note:

- **Only DLS can authorize** travel accommodations — Amazon Travel & Events and your manager cannot approve these requests.
- Visit the [DLS site](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) for information on the process and requirements.
- If you are a **Candidate or New Hire**, contact the **Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)** instead.

You can also refer to **section 3.5 Travelers with Additional Needs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for further details.

rows.query: how do I set up my travel profile for the first time
rows.answer:
## Setting Up Your Travel Profile

To set up your Travel Profile for the first time:

1. **Go to the Self-Service Booking Tool:** https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/
2. **Create your profile**, which will store:
   - Personal information (mobile phone number, date of birth)
   - Passport details (for international travel)
   - Corporate or personal credit card (required for hotel reservations)
   - Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

3. **Verify your name** — some information will be pre-populated, but confirm your name **exactly matches your government-issued ID**.

---

**Quick Start Guides** (select the one relevant to your region):
- **Global:** [Traveler Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/NewTravelSystem/) | [Travel Arranger Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Travel_Arranger_NewTravelsystem/)
- **India, China, Japan, or Regional** locations also have dedicated guides available.

---

> 📌 **India employees only:** If your full name exceeds 58 characters, you must book through a Travel Counselor instead of online.

For policy details, refer to **section 3.4 Traveler Profiles** in the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec).

rows.query: do I get to keep my frequent flyer miles when I travel for work
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can earn and keep personal loyalty miles when traveling for business through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). This applies to airlines, hotels, and rental cars.

**To make sure you receive your miles:**
- Sign up for loyalty programs and add your loyalty numbers to your **Travel Profile**
- Ensure your Travel Profile name **exactly matches** your loyalty program name
- Miles are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip
- If miles aren't credited, contact the travel provider directly

**Note:** Employees based in **Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel** are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

rows.query: I got into a car accident in a rental during a work trip, what now
rows.answer:
Here's what you need to do:

1. **Contact emergency services first** if there are any injuries or immediate safety concerns.

2. **Report the damage** by visiting [Amazon Travel Risk Management](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) for the damage reporting process.

3. **Inform your manager** once the above steps are completed.

For coverage, since you were in a rental booked through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool with a preferred supplier, Amazon provides **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)** insurance — though note that deductibles may apply depending on your rental location.

For full details, refer to:
- [Section 6.8 Car Accidents](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#62c58a98-3514-2159-75a6-abd568753d90) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy
- [Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#88c58a98-3515-6808-3fb9-b6456aec9e1b) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy

rows.query: do I need a visa to travel internationally for business
rows.answer:
You must complete a **pre-travel immigration assessment** through the **International Business Travel Guided Experience** before traveling internationally. This assessment determines your specific visa and immigration requirements based on:

- Your destination country
- Your citizenship
- Trip duration and purpose

For full details, refer to **Section 3.9 Visas and Passports** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy: https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9

rows.query: can I pay extra myself to upgrade on a really long flight
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — there are actually two options available to you:

1. **Pay out of pocket for an upgrade** (available to all employees): You can upgrade your fare class by paying the difference between the policy-approved economy fare and the upgraded class yourself. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy. See [Context 2/4] for booking steps.

2. **Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program** — if your flight is **14 hours or more** (excluding layovers), you may qualify to book premium economy at Amazon's expense if you:
   - Book all travel through Amazon Travel & Events, **and**
   - Have completed **4 or more one-way trips exceeding 14 hours** in the last 12 months.

   If you meet those criteria, you're automatically added to the PEA list — no action needed. You can check your PEA status via [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91).

Additionally, you can also use **personal loyalty miles** to upgrade after booking, by contacting the airline directly — though eligibility depends on your fare class and airline rules.

rows.query: is it ok to add a few personal vacation days onto a work trip
rows.answer:
Yes, this is allowed. You can extend business trips to add personal time, but you need **line manager approval before extending your trip**.

Keep in mind:
- Amazon only reimburses costs related to the **business portion** of the trip
- Any personal expenses during the extension are **your responsibility**

For more details, refer to section 3.6 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need my manager to approve my travel before I book
rows.answer:
**No**, — manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Your manager will automatically receive a copy of your itinerary once your booking is complete, but this is **for awareness only** and does not require any action from them.

rows.query: do I need to buy insurance for a rental car on a business trip
rows.answer:
**No, you do not need to purchase additional rental car insurance for business travel.**

Amazon provides the following coverage when you book through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) using preferred suppliers:
- **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)**
- **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)**

**At the rental counter:** Decline any additional insurance offered, unless it is legally required and not already included in your rate.

Note: Deductibles may apply depending on the rental location.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: does the company provide travel insurance when I fly
rows.answer:
Yes. Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for business travel. You do **not** need to purchase separate travel insurance.

For details on coverage and how to file claims, visit the [Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program](https://inside.hr.amazon.dev/content/inside/us/en/employment/your-career-global/insurance-and-assistance-program.html) page, or refer to **section 10.4** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: the booking tool is broken, who do I contact for tech help
rows.answer: Go to **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to get technical support for the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool. You can use it to resolve login problems, system errors, payment display issues, or anything else preventing you from completing a booking.

rows.query: how do I reach a travel counselor or agent
rows.answer:
## Reaching a Travel Counselor

Here are the available contact options:

- **Phone:** Call the number listed in your **Travel Profile** (using your country-specific number ensures proper routing and identification)
- **Chat:** Available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries *(note: chat does not support new reservations)*
- **Email:** Available as an option — find details on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/)

Travel Counselors are available **24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries.

### Tips for faster service:
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- Ensure your **Travel Profile** has your current phone number
- Departing **within 72 hours?** You're automatically moved to the top of the call queue
- Don't want to wait on hold? Use the **Callback Feature**

### Traveling internationally?
If you don't have an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead of calling.

rows.query: can I book a personal vacation through the work travel tool
rows.answer:
**No.** The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) is for **business travel only**. Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business use, and personal trips are not permitted in the tool.

For personal travel, visit **Amazon Extras** (extrasforamazon.com) for leisure travel promotions and booking options.

rows.query: what hotels am I allowed to book and what is the nightly limit
rows.answer:
## Hotel Booking Guidelines

You are allowed to book the **lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis**, close to your work destination, through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or via a Travel Counselor.

**Preferred hotels** are recommended as they offer competitive rates, flexible cancellation policies, loyalty program eligibility, and have been vetted for safety and security.

**For stays of 7+ nights** in the same location, Corporate Serviced Apartments are an option (studio or one-bedroom, whichever is less expensive), but require L8+ approval.

---

Regarding a **nightly rate limit** — I don't have that information in the available context. I'd recommend checking the [Global Travel and Expense Policy Section 5.0](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) or contacting a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for clarification.

rows.query: why is the Amazon payment option not showing when I book a flight
rows.answer:
The Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution) is unavailable when your booking includes charges that are **not eligible for central billing**. Common reasons include:

- **Personal upgrades** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Seat selection fees**
- **Baggage fees**
- **Other ancillary charges**
- **Personal travel segments**

Additionally, some airlines require **multi-factor authentication** for payment processing, which also requires an alternate form of payment.

In these cases, you must pay using an **alternate payment method**.

If none of these conditions apply and you believe you should have access, contact [Amazon Travel Support](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

rows.query: I got a hotel confirmation email I never asked for, why
rows.answer:
This is expected behavior from Amazon Travel & Events. The system uses **automated auditing technology** that monitors your hotel reservations after booking and automatically **rebooks your hotel when a lower rate is found**.

Here's what you should know:

- **Nothing changes** about your reservation (same property, dates, room type)
- **What does change** is your confirmation number and rate (which will be lower)
- **No action is needed** from you

Just make sure to **use the new confirmation number** when checking in or contacting the hotel.

rows.query: I was charged for a hotel even though I cancelled it
rows.answer:
If you cancelled through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** and were still charged, contact **Amazon Travel Support** to investigate.

**Before reaching out, have the following ready:**
- Confirmation that your cancellation was within the hotel's cancellation period
- Date and time of your cancellation
- Receipt from the hotel for the no-show charge

You can reach Amazon Travel Support at: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

rows.query: how do I cancel or change a hotel reservation
rows.answer:
## Cancelling or Modifying a Hotel Reservation

Use the **[Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)** to make most changes.

**Before modifying, confirm all of the following:**
- You have **not** checked into the hotel
- Your request is within the **cancellation policy guidelines**
- The hotel has **not** been paid for in advance

If your reservation doesn't meet these requirements, contact a **[Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/)**.

---

**To modify your hotel:**
1. Log in to the Self-Service Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Find your hotel reservation and select **Modify** (or **Cancel**)
4. Update check-in/check-out dates or select a different room type/rate
5. Review changes and any price differences, then select **Book Hotel** to confirm

**Important tip:** If booking a new hotel, **book the new reservation first**, then cancel the old one — this ensures you don't lose your spot if the preferred hotel sells out.

For additional help, refer to **[Section 3.10 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10)**.

rows.query: which expenses can I actually get reimbursed for
rows.answer:
Based on the context, Amazon reimburses **business expenses that are approved, necessary to your job, and reflect good judgment and Frugality**. Here are key reimbursable categories:

**Travel & Transportation**
- Airfare, hotel, car rental (must be booked through Amazon's TMC)
- Train, local transportation (taxis, Uber, Lyft), personal mileage
- Business parking

**Meals**
- Individual travel meals, group travel meals
- Business meals with clients or government officials (with restrictions)

**Communications**
- Mobile phone base plan – up to **$50/month** (L4-L8)
- Incremental mobile/travel fees (roaming, hotspot) – up to **$150/month**

**Other Common Expenses**
- Office supplies, software, shipping
- Training and professional development
- Laundry during travel, tips/gratuities, passport/visa fees
- Currency exchange fees
- Vaccinations for international travel

**Important notes:**
- Always **consult the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication)** before making a purchase
- Manager or PXT approval **does not** override policy
- Switzerland-based employees must also follow the [Switzerland T&E Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/703733/publication)
- Non-Amazon employees (contractors, candidates) follow a separate [Non-Employee Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/610963/publication)

rows.query: what is the deadline to submit my expenses after a trip
rows.answer: You must submit your expense report within **60 days of the transaction date**. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses and corporate card transactions. Missing this deadline for corporate card transactions may result in your card being suspended.

rows.query: how do I file an expense report
rows.answer:
## How to Submit an Expense Report

You can find step-by-step guidance in the user guides for either expense tool:

- **Emburse (new tool):** [How to Submit Expenses - Emburse](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)
- **Concur (legacy tool):** [How to Submit Expenses - Concur](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide)

You can also search the **[Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us)** for video demos and how-to guides.

> **Note:** Amazon is currently migrating from Concur to Emburse. Visit the **Expense Tool Migration Hub** to check the timeline for your location.

**Key reminder:** Submit your expense report within **60 days** of the transaction date to avoid corporate card suspension.

rows.query: my expense report got rejected, what do I do now
rows.answer:
## Your Rejected Expense Report: Next Steps

1. **Check the Expense Tool** at [expenses.corp.amazon.com](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) — your rejected report will be returned there with **guidance on how to fix and resubmit** it.

2. **Common fixes you may be asked to make:**
   - Submit legible receipts
   - Provide additional justification
   - Select a different expense type

3. **If you believe it was rejected in error:** Review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication), then add a detailed justification in the **comment field** and resubmit.

4. **If additional approvals are needed:** Your manager or skip-level manager will be added as a reviewer — no action is needed from you unless you want to follow up with them directly.

> **Need help resubmitting?** Contact the Amazon Global Expense Team through the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

**Note:** The 60-day submission deadline clock does **not restart** if you need to resubmit a rejected report.

rows.query: I can't figure out which expense category to use
rows.answer:
Use the **Expense Type Guide** to find the right category:

- **Concur users:** https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/
- **Emburse users:** https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide

The guide contains detailed descriptions of every available expense type, including country-specific restrictions.

If you don't see the expense type you need in the Expense Tool, it likely isn't available based on your location.

For additional help, you can also visit the **Expense Tool Help Center** (https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) or the **Expense & Corporate Card RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: are meals paid for while I'm traveling for business
rows.answer:
Yes, meals are covered during business travel. Amazon reimburses meals including breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks, and related food and beverage costs incurred while traveling away from home for business.

**Key requirements:**
- Retain your **itemized receipt** showing individual meal items to submit with your expense report
- Use the **"Individual Travel Meal"** expense type when submitting

**For group meals while traveling:**
- The most senior person should pay and list all attendees when submitting
- Only split the bill if each person plans to expense their own meal separately

For specific per diem rates and regional expense limits, visit the [Travel Reimbursement Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/).

> **Note:** If your meal includes a government official, additional rules apply — review the Gift Reporting Guidelines and consult your manager before expensing.

rows.query: can I expense my cell phone bill
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — Amazon reimburses the business portion of your personal mobile phone base plan, up to **$50 USD/month** (for employees L4–L8; exceptions apply for L1–L3 and L10+).

**How to submit:**
1. Go to the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/)
2. Select **"Mobile Phone"** as the Expense Type
3. Enter your total base plan cost
4. Select your business use percentage:
   - 25% – infrequent use
   - 50% – mixed use
   - 75% – significant use
   - 100% – primarily work use
5. Attach your **full itemized bill** showing your name and/or phone number

The system will automatically calculate your reimbursement, capped at $50.

**Additional travel-related mobile costs** (e.g., international roaming, mobile hotspots) can be reimbursed up to **$150/month** using the **"Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees"** Expense Type.

> Review Section 12.6 of the [Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting to confirm your eligibility.

rows.query: how long until I actually get my reimbursement money
rows.answer:
Most expense reports are processed and paid within **4 business days**.

However, it may take longer if:
- Your report has been **flagged for additional review** (likely if it's been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days)
- Your report **requires additional approvals** from your manager or skip-level manager

To avoid delays, review the [Expense Report Checklist](https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) before submitting to ensure your report is complete, compliant, and accurate.

rows.query: I lost the receipt for something, can I still expense it
rows.answer:
Yes, you have three options if you're missing a receipt:

1. **Contact the merchant for a new receipt** – This is the preferred option and the **only acceptable solution** for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals.

2. **Submit a bank/card statement** – Include your name, transaction amount, and date (redact personal details and unrelated charges). This requires **manager approval**.

3. **Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD)** – A last resort for **low-value expenses only**. Not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete this yourself (delegates cannot) by checking "Missing Receipt" when creating the expense in the Expense Tool. Also requires **manager approval**, and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access.

rows.query: I accidentally paid for a personal thing on my corporate card
rows.answer:
**Here's what to do:**

1. Find the corporate card charge in the **Expense Tool** under your eWallet.
2. Select the Expense Type **"Personal Expense"** (since the entire amount is personal/non-reimbursable).
3. Fill out the required fields.
4. Submit the expense report.

**What happens next:** By submitting as a personal expense, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or your account in Amazon Touchstone.

⚠️ **Note:** Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension, so be careful going forward.

If you need help navigating the Expense Tool, refer to the [How to Submit Expenses user guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses).

rows.query: who do I talk to if I need help with an expense report
rows.answer:
You have several options for getting help with an expense report:

- **Self-service:** Visit the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) to solve common issues or submit a ticket to the right team.
- **Submission guidance:** Search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for video demos and how-to guides.
- **Community support:** Join the unofficial **#expenses-at-amazon-interest** Slack channel for advice from fellow Amazonians.
- **Direct assistance:** Contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: can I upgrade my fare using points or miles instead of paying
rows.answer:
Yes, you can upgrade your fare using personal loyalty miles. Here's what you need to know:

- **Book your travel first** through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- **Then contact the airline directly** to request the mileage upgrade — this **cannot** be processed through the booking tool

**Key limitations to be aware of:**
- Not all airlines allow mileage upgrades for tickets booked through third-party platforms
- Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically **not eligible**
- Review your specific fare rules before requesting an upgrade

For more details, refer to **section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: why do I have to book through the official travel program at all
rows.answer:
Booking through Amazon Travel & Events is **required** for several key reasons:

1. **Safety & Security** – 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details, plus automatic registration for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.

2. **Cost Savings** – Access to exclusive corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are **not available** on consumer booking sites.

3. **Booking Simplicity** – Access to pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.

4. **Sustainability** – Supports Amazon's Climate Pledge commitments by offering lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotel options.

5. **Service Quality** – Pre-screened providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

Note: Use of external booking methods is **strictly prohibited**, except when no availability exists through the Travel Management Company (TMC).

rows.query: where is the contact info for travel support in my region
rows.answer:
You can find Travel Counselor contact information — including **phone, email, chat, and hours by country** — on the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki**:
👉 https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

**Tips before contacting:**
- Call from the phone number listed in your **Travel Profile** for proper routing
- Have your **Trip ID** ready for faster service
- **Chat** is also available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (note: chat does not support new reservations)
- If traveling internationally without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead

rows.query: what do I do if my flight gets cancelled by the airline
rows.answer:
Based on the available context, I don't have specific information about the process when an **airline cancels your flight**.

However, the context does suggest that if online self-service changes are not available for your booking, you should **contact a Travel Counselor** for assistance: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

You can also visit the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki** for more information: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

rows.query: are baggage fees and seat selection reimbursable when I travel
rows.answer:
**Yes, generally — but with conditions.**

**Seat Assignment & Baggage:**
You should book the **Lowest Logical Fare that includes a seat assignment and baggage**. These are considered part of your in-policy fare.

**For EMEA and APAC travelers specifically:**
If fares that include a standard seat or baggage exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits, you should:
- Purchase the seat or baggage **separately** as an ancillary item directly with the airline
- Expense the additional cost separately
- Save all receipts for ancillary purchases

**What is NOT reimbursable:**
- **Upgrade fees** and **premium seating fees** are explicitly not reimbursable
- You are only reimbursed for the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class

For full details, refer to **section 4.7 Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: how do I book a flight and hotel for a business trip
rows.answer:
## Booking a Flight and Hotel for a Business Trip

Here's how to get started:

### 1. Set Up Your Travel Profile (if you haven't already)
Before your first booking, create your **Travel Profile** at the Self-Service Booking Tool. It stores your personal info, passport details, and payment method (required for hotel reservations).

### 2. Book Your Travel
- **Standard bookings:** Log in to the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights and hotels.
- **Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure):** Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours.

### First Time Booking?
Review the **New Hire Travel Playbook** at https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/newhire_playbook/ for a complete onboarding guide.

For full policy details, refer to section 3.3 of the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#66c58a98-3506-6b1f-2814-abf4f9de7f38).

rows.query: can I fly business class and how much will the company pay back
rows.answer:
**Flying Business Class**

Yes, you can upgrade to business class, but **Amazon will only reimburse the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class**. You must pay the difference out of pocket.

**How it works:**
- Pay the full upgraded ticket cost upfront using your own payment method
- Obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and business class
- When expensing, claim **only the Lowest Logical Fare** amount for reimbursement
- Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are **not reimbursable**

**Note:** Your manager will receive an email notification about the personal upgrade, but no action is required from them.

rows.query: what happens if my booking comes up as out of policy
rows.answer:
## Out of Policy Bookings

An **"Out of Policy"** flag identifies options that exceed policy guidelines, but **it does not prevent you from completing your booking**.

### Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation
- **Out of Policy**: Your selection exceeds guidelines but *can* be justified for business reasons — you will be prompted to provide a reason.
- **Policy Violation**: Your selection breaks a hard rule (e.g., booking first class) — the booking *cannot* be completed.

### When You Can Proceed
You may book out of policy when there is a valid business reason, such as:
- A hotel that is closer to your business meetings
- Renting an SUV during inclement weather for safety reasons

### What to Do
Simply **provide a brief reason when prompted**. This acts as an audit tool to balance flexibility with frugality.

For full details, refer to the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#1).

rows.query: where do I find a trip I booked a few weeks ago
rows.answer:
Go to the **Self-Service Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and select **Trips** (or **Trips Library** depending on your region) from the main menu — this shows both current and past trips, including invoices.

If the trip is no longer visible or has been archived, submit a request through **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records.

rows.query: what size rental car am I allowed to rent for work
rows.answer:
You are allowed to book **up to intermediate size vehicles** (mini, economy, or compact) through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Exceptions allowing larger vehicles include:**
- **Electric/hybrid vehicles** – If only standard or full-size electric/hybrid options are available, you may book those
- **3+ travelers** sharing the same rental vehicle
- **Large work equipment** requiring additional cargo space
- **Pre-approved medical reasons**
- **Safety exceptions** (e.g., AWD/4WD in winter/blizzard-prone locations)

For full details, refer to [Section 6.1 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#6).

rows.query: I need to move my flight to a different day, how do I change it
rows.answer:
## How to Modify Your Flight

You can change your flight using the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** at [booktravel.corp.amazon.com](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

**Steps to modify your flight:**
1. Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Select your segment and select **Modify**
4. Choose the flight to change
5. Update your details and search for new flights
6. Complete checkout

If self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a **Travel Counselor** for assistance.

You can also watch this quick video for guidance: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156)

rows.query: what kinds of flights am I actually allowed to book
rows.answer:
You must book the **Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class** with a seat assignment through the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Key points:
- **Non-stop flights** are preferred over connecting flights when possible and practical
- If a non-stop flight is **not** the Lowest Logical Fare, consult your manager to balance frugality and well-being
- **Premium Economy** is only available if you qualify for the Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program (requires 4+ one-way trips of 14+ hours in the last 12 months)

All bookings should use preferred suppliers identified in the booking tool when available.

rows.query: how do I pick or change my seat on a flight
rows.answer:
## Selecting or Changing Your Seat

**During booking:**
- Seat maps are available during the shopping path to compare availability across flights.
- A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout, but you can choose a different seat by viewing the seat map before completing your booking.

**After booking:**
You can change your seat once ticketed by:
- Navigating to **Trips > My Trips** in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- Using the **airline mobile app** directly (varies by airline)

For a visual walkthrough, watch: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

> **Note for EMEA/APAC travelers:** If a fare with a seat included exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, purchase the seat separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense it separately, and save your receipt.

rows.query: I have a medical condition, how do I request a travel accommodation
rows.answer:
Contact **Disability and Leave Services (DLS)** to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment or limitation. **Only DLS can authorize travel accommodations** — Amazon Travel & Events and your manager cannot.

**How to request:**
- Visit the [DLS site](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) for process details and requirements.
- If you are a **candidate or new hire**, contact the **Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)** instead.

You can also refer to **section 3.5 Travelers with Additional Needs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for further guidance.

rows.query: how do I set up my travel profile for the first time
rows.answer:
## Setting Up Your Travel Profile

To set up your Travel Profile for the first time:

1. Go to the **Self-Service Booking Tool**: https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/
2. Create your profile, which will store:
   - Personal information (mobile phone number and date of birth)
   - Passport details (for international travel)
   - Corporate or personal credit card (required for hotel reservations)
   - Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

> **Important:** Some information will be pre-populated — verify that your **name exactly matches your government-issued ID**.

---

### Quick Start Guides (select your region):
- **Global Access:** [Traveler Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/NewTravelSystem/)
- **India:** [Traveler Guide](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FProfile%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1)
- **Japan:** [Quick Start Videos](https://broadcast.amazon.com/channels/1303/playlists/18326)
- **China:** [Traveler Guide](https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripSouce%20China%5FBCD%20Quick%20Start%20Guide%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FChina%20Public&p=true&ga=1)

For policy details

rows.query: do I get to keep my frequent flyer miles when I travel for work
rows.answer:
**Yes**, — you can keep any personal loyalty miles and points earned when traveling for business through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). This applies to airlines, hotels, and rental cars.

**To make sure you earn your miles:**
- Sign up for loyalty programs and add your loyalty numbers to your **Travel Profile**
- Ensure your Travel Profile name **exactly matches** your loyalty program name
- Miles are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip

**Note:** Employees based in **Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel** are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

If miles are not credited after your trip, contact the travel provider directly.

rows.query: I got into a car accident in a rental during a work trip, what now
rows.answer:
Here's what you need to do:

1. **Contact emergency services first** if there are injuries or immediate safety concerns.
2. **Report the damage** by visiting [Amazon Travel Risk Management](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) for the damage reporting process.
3. **Inform your manager** once the above steps are completed.

For full details, refer to [section 6.8 Car Accidents](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#62c58a98-3514-2159-75a6-abd568753d90) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

**Regarding insurance coverage:** If you booked through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) using a preferred supplier, Amazon provides both Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) coverage. Note that deductibles may apply depending on your rental location. See [section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#88c58a98-3515-6808-3fb9-b6456aec9e1b) for more details.

rows.query: do I need a visa to travel internationally for business
rows.answer:
Yes, you are required to complete a **pre-travel immigration assessment** through the **International Business Travel Guided Experience** before traveling internationally. This assessment will determine your specific visa and immigration requirements based on:

- Your **destination country**
- Your **citizenship**
- Your **trip details** (duration and purpose)

For more information, refer to **section 3.9 Visas and Passports** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: can I pay extra myself to upgrade on a really long flight
rows.answer:
**Yes, you have two options depending on your situation:**

**1. Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) Program** – If you've completed **4+ one-way flights over 14 hours** in the last 12 months (all booked through Amazon Travel), you may automatically qualify for Amazon-approved premium economy booking at no personal cost. Check your status via [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91).

**2. Personal Upgrade (Out of Pocket)** – If you don't qualify for PEA, you can still upgrade by paying the difference yourself:
- Amazon reimburses only the **Lowest Logical Fare** in economy.
- You pay the upgrade cost out of pocket.
- Only expense the policy-approved economy fare amount.
- Note: **Upgrade fees are not reimbursable.**
- Your manager will receive an automatic notification, but no action is required from them.

The booking process varies slightly depending on whether you have **Global Access** (self-service booking tool) or **Regional** access (contact a Travel Counselor).

rows.query: is it ok to add a few personal vacation days onto a work trip
rows.answer:
Yes, this is allowed. You can extend business trips to add personal time, but you need **line manager approval before extending your trip**.

Key points to keep in mind:
- Amazon only reimburses costs related to the **business portion** of the trip
- Any personal expenses during the extension are **your responsibility**

For more details, refer to section 3.6 Extended Trip for Personal Time in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: do I need my manager to approve my travel before I book
rows.answer:
**No**, — manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Your manager will automatically receive your itinerary once you complete a booking, but this is for **awareness only** and requires no action from them.

rows.query: do I need to buy insurance for a rental car on a business trip
rows.answer:
**No, you do not need to purchase additional rental car insurance for business trips.**

Amazon provides the following coverage when you book through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) using preferred suppliers:
- **Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)**
- **Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)**

**At the rental counter:** Decline any additional insurance offered, unless it is legally required and not already included in your rate.

**Note:** Deductibles may apply depending on the rental location.

For full details, refer to **Section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: does the company provide travel insurance when I fly
rows.answer:
Yes. Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for all business travel — you do **not** need to purchase separate travel insurance.

For details on coverage and how to file claims, visit the [Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program](https://inside.hr.amazon.dev/content/inside/us/en/employment/your-career-global/insurance-and-assistance-program.html) page, or refer to **Section 10.4** of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: the booking tool is broken, who do I contact for tech help
rows.answer: Access **Travel RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to get technical support for the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool. You can use it to resolve login problems, system errors, payment display issues, or anything else preventing you from completing a booking.

rows.query: how do I reach a travel counselor or agent
rows.answer:
## Reaching a Travel Counselor

Here are the ways to contact a Travel Counselor:

- **Phone:** Call from the number listed in your **Travel Profile** to ensure proper call routing and identification. Find the country-specific phone number on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).
- **Chat:** Available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries. *(Note: Chat does not support new reservations.)*
- **Email:** Also available — details found on the Travel & Events Wiki.

**Tips for faster service:**
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- Ensure your Travel Profile has your current phone number
- If departing within **72 hours**, you are automatically prioritized to the top of the call queue
- Use the **Callback Feature** to avoid waiting on hold

**Traveling internationally?** If you don't have an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead.

Travel Counselors are available **24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries.

rows.query: can I book a personal vacation through the work travel tool
rows.answer:
No. The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool is for **business travel only**. Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business travel and cannot be used for personal trips.

For personal travel, visit **Amazon Extras** (https://extrasforamazon.com/app/home) for leisure travel promotions and personal booking options.

rows.query: what hotels am I allowed to book and what is the nightly limit
rows.answer:
## Hotel Booking Policy

You are allowed to book the **lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis**, close to your work destination, through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or a Travel Counselor. Amazon preferred hotels are recommended as they offer competitive rates and flexible cancellation policies.

**Regarding a nightly rate limit** — I don't have that information in the available context. You may want to consult the [Global Travel and Expense Policy Section 5.0](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) or contact your Travel Counselor for details on any nightly spending caps.

rows.query: why is the Amazon payment option not showing when I book a flight
rows.answer:
The Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution) is unavailable when your booking includes charges not eligible for central billing. Common reasons include:

- **Personal upgrades** (e.g., premium cabin, extra legroom)
- **Seat selection fees**
- **Baggage fees**
- **Other ancillary charges**
- **Personal travel segments**

Additionally, some airlines require **multi-factor authentication**, which also requires an alternate payment method.

For any of these cases, you'll need to pay using an alternate payment method.

If none of these conditions apply and you believe you should have access, contact [Amazon Travel Support](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

rows.query: I got a hotel confirmation email I never asked for, why
rows.answer:
This is from Amazon Travel & Events' automated auditing system, which monitors your hotel reservations for lower rates. When a better rate is found, it automatically rebooks your hotel to capture savings.

**What stays the same:**
- Hotel property, check-in/check-out dates, room type, and preferences

**What changes:**
- Your confirmation number and your rate (it will be lower)

**No action is needed from you.** Just make sure to use the **new confirmation number** when checking in or contacting the hotel.

rows.query: I was charged for a hotel even though I cancelled it
rows.answer:
If you cancelled through the **Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** and were still charged, contact **Amazon Travel Support** to investigate.

Before reaching out, make sure you have the following ready:
- Confirmation that cancellation was **within the hotel's cancellation period**
- The **date and time** of your cancellation
- The **receipt** from the hotel for the no-show charge

Contact Amazon Travel Support here: https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

rows.query: how do I cancel or change a hotel reservation
rows.answer:
## Cancelling or Modifying a Hotel Reservation

Use the **[Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)** for most hotel changes.

### Before you begin, confirm all of the following:
- You have **not** checked into the hotel
- Your request is **within the cancellation policy guidelines**
- The hotel has **not been paid for in advance**

If your reservation doesn't meet these requirements, contact a **[Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/)**.

---

### How to Modify/Cancel (Step-by-Step):
1. Log in to the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
2. Navigate to **Trips > Upcoming** and select your trip
3. Find your hotel reservation and select **Modify** or **Cancel**
4. Make your changes (dates, room type, etc.) and confirm on the Checkout page

> **Tip:** If booking a new hotel, **book the new reservation first**, then cancel the old one — this prevents losing your spot if the preferred hotel sells out.

---

For policy details, refer to **[Section 3.10 – Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10)**.

You can also watch this video: **[Modify Your Flight & Hotel Booking](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156)**.

rows.query: which expenses can I actually get reimbursed for
rows.answer:
## Reimbursable Expenses at Amazon

Amazon reimburses **business expenses that are approved, necessary to your job, and reflect good judgment and Frugality**. Here are the main categories:

### Travel & Transportation
- Airfare, train, car rental, hotel/lodging
- Local transportation (taxis, Uber/Lyft, tolls)
- Personal car mileage (for business travel)
- Business parking

### Meals & Incidentals
- Individual travel meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner while traveling)
- Group business meals during travel
- Tips/gratuities (where culturally appropriate)
- Laundry during business travel
- Currency exchange fees
- Passport/visa fees for business travel

### Communications
- **Mobile phone** base plan: up to **$50/month**
- **Incremental mobile fees** (roaming, hotspot, etc.): up to **$150/month**

### Other Common Expenses
- Office supplies
- Training & professional development
- Professional dues/subscriptions
- Equipment below CAPEX threshold

---

**Important reminders:**
- Always **check the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication)** before purchasing
- **Manager approval alone does not override policy**
- Some expense types are **region-specific or role-restricted**

For full details, consult your applicable policy before submitting.

rows.query: what is the deadline to submit my expenses after a trip
rows.answer: You must submit your expense report within **60 days of the transaction date**. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses and corporate card transactions. Note that if you fail to submit corporate card transactions within 60 days, your corporate card may be suspended.

rows.query: how do I file an expense report
rows.answer:
## How to Submit an Expense Report

Amazon is currently migrating from **Concur** to **Emburse**, so the tool you use depends on your location. Here's how to get started:

1. **Check which tool applies to you** – Visit the [Expense Tool Migration Hub] to confirm your location's timeline.

2. **Follow the step-by-step guides:**
   - **Concur users:** [Concur User Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide)
   - **Emburse users:** [How to Submit Expenses](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)

3. **Additional resources:**
   - Search the [Expense Tool Help Center](https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for video demos and how-to guides.
   - Visit the [Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) for self-service support.

> **Reminder:** Submit your expense report within **60 days** of the transaction date to avoid potential corporate card suspension.

rows.query: my expense report got rejected, what do I do now
rows.answer:
## Your Rejected Expense Report: Next Steps

Here's what to do:

1. **Check the Expense Tool** (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) — Your rejected report will be returned there with guidance on how to fix it. You may need to:
   - Submit legible receipts
   - Provide additional justification
   - Select a different expense type

2. **Review the policy** — Check the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication). If you believe the rejection was an error, add a detailed justification in the **comment field** and resubmit.

3. **If additional approvals are needed** — Your manager or skip-level may be added as a reviewer. No action is required from you unless you want to follow up with them directly.

4. **Need help resubmitting?** — Contact the Amazon Global Expense Team via the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

> **Note:** If you need to resubmit, the 60-day submission deadline clock does **not** restart from the rejection date.

rows.query: I can't figure out which expense category to use
rows.answer:
Use the **Expense Type Guide** to find the right category:

- **Concur users:** https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/
- **Emburse users:** https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide

The guide contains detailed descriptions of every available expense type, including country-specific restrictions.

**Note:** If you can't find a specific expense type in the Expense Tool, it likely isn't available for your location.

If you still need help, visit the **Expense & Corporate Card RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) for additional support.

rows.query: are meals paid for while I'm traveling for business
rows.answer:
**Yes, meals are covered during business travel.**

Amazon reimburses meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business. A few key rules to follow:

- **Keep itemized receipts** that show individual meal items to submit with your expense report.
- **For group/team meals**, the most senior person should pay and list all attendees when submitting.
- Only split the bill if each person plans to expense their own meal separately.
- Use the **"Individual Travel Meal"** expense type for solo meals, or **"Travel Business Meals - Group"** for group meals while traveling.

For specific per diem rates and regional expense limits, visit the [Travel Reimbursement Guide](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/).

> **Note for Germany-based employees:** You must submit for a tax-free per diem instead of expensing individual meals.

rows.query: can I expense my cell phone bill
rows.answer:
**Yes, you can expense your cell phone bill** (for employees L4–L8).

Here's how:

- **Reimbursement cap:** Up to **$50 USD/month** for your personal mobile phone base plan
- **Where to submit:** Go to the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) and select **"Mobile Phone"** as the Expense Type
- **What to enter:** Your total base plan cost and your business use percentage:
  - 25% – infrequent use
  - 50% – mixed use
  - 75% – significant use
  - 100% – primarily work use
- **Required documentation:** Attach your full **itemized bill** showing your name and/or phone number

**Additional charges (e.g., international roaming, mobile hotspot):** Submit separately using the **"Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees"** Expense Type — reimbursable up to **$150/month** with detailed line-item documentation.

> **Note:** Exceptions apply for L1–L3 and L10+. Review [Section 12.6 of the Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting to confirm your eligibility.

rows.query: how long until I actually get my reimbursement money
rows.answer:
Most expense reports are processed and paid within **4 business days**. However, it may take longer if:

- Your report has been **flagged for additional review** (likely if it's been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days)
- Your report **requires additional approvals** from your manager or skip-level manager

To avoid delays, review the [Expense Report Checklist](https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) before submitting to ensure your report is complete, compliant, and accurate.

rows.query: I lost the receipt for something, can I still expense it
rows.answer:
Yes, you have three options if you're missing a receipt:

1. **Contact the merchant for a new receipt** – This is the best option and the **only acceptable solution for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals**.

2. **Submit a bank/card statement** – If you can't get a merchant receipt, submit a statement showing your name, transaction amount, and date (redact personal details). This requires **manager approval**.

3. **Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD)** – A last resort for **low-value expenses only**. Not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete this yourself (delegates cannot) by checking "Missing Receipt" in the Expense Tool. Also requires **manager approval**, and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access.

rows.query: I accidentally paid for a personal thing on my corporate card
rows.answer:
**No worries — here's what to do:**

1. **Find the charge** in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet.
2. **Select "Personal Expense"** as the Expense Type (if the entire amount is personal).
3. **Fill out the required fields**, then submit the expense report.

By submitting, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from a future reimbursement or your account in Amazon Touchstone.

**⚠️ Note:** Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension.

---

**If only part of the transaction was personal:**
- Apply the appropriate expense type for the business portion.
- Under **Allocation**, click **Add Allocation** and select **"PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense"**, then adjust the amounts accordingly.

For additional help, visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

rows.query: who do I talk to if I need help with an expense report
