rows.answer:
Contact the **Amazon Global Expense Team** through the **Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER** (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).

You can also explore these self-service options first:
- **Expense Tool Help Center** (https://help.chromeriver.com/hc/en-us) for guides and video demos
- The **#expenses-at-amazon-interest** Slack channel for peer advice

rows.query: can I upgrade my fare using points or miles instead of paying
rows.answer:
Yes, you can upgrade your fare using personal loyalty miles. Here's what you need to know:

- **Book your travel first** through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)
- **Contact the airline directly** to request the mileage upgrade — it cannot be processed through the booking tool

**Important limitations to be aware of:**
- Not all airlines allow mileage upgrades for tickets booked through third-party platforms
- Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically **not eligible**
- Review your specific fare rules before requesting an upgrade

For more details, refer to **Section 3.12 Loyalty Programs** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

rows.query: why do I have to book through the official travel program at all
rows.answer:
Booking through Amazon Travel & Events is required for several key reasons:

- **Safety & Security**: 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details, plus automatic registration for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.
- **Cost Savings**: Access to exclusive corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are unavailable on consumer booking sites.
- **Booking Simplicity**: Access to pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.
- **Sustainability**: Supports Amazon's Climate Pledge commitment by offering lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotel options.
- **Service Quality**: Pre-screened travel providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

These benefits are not available through consumer booking sites.

rows.query: where is the contact info for travel support in my region
rows.answer:
You can find Travel Counselor contact information — including **phone, email, chat, and hours by country** — on the **Amazon Travel & Events Wiki**:
👉 https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/

**Tips before contacting:**
- Call from the phone number in your **Travel Profile** for proper routing
- Have your **Trip ID** ready for faster service
- If departing within **72 hours**, you'll be automatically prioritized in the call queue
- **Chat** is also available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (note: chat does not support new reservations)
- If traveling internationally without an international calling plan, use **chat or email** instead

rows.query: what do I do if my flight gets cancelled by the airline
rows.answer:
If your flight is cancelled by the airline, here's what you can do:

1. **Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool** (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to rebook your trip, as it supports modifications for most bookings.

2. **If self-service changes are not available** for your booking, contact a **Travel Counselor** for assistance. They are available **24/7, 365 days a year** in most countries. Find their contact information (phone, email, chat) at the Amazon Travel & Events Wiki (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).

**Tips for faster service when calling:**
- Have your **Trip ID** ready
- Ensure your Travel Profile has your current phone number
- If you're departing within **72 hours**, you are automatically prioritized in the call queue
- If you don't want to wait on hold, use the **Callback Feature**

Note: I don't have specific information about airline-initiated cancellation procedures beyond these general steps. For policy details, refer to **section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations** in the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10).

rows.query: are baggage fees and seat selection reimbursable when I travel
rows.answer:
**Yes, baggage fees and seat selection are generally reimbursable** when booking the Lowest Logical Fare — here's how it works:

**Best approach:** Book a fare that **includes** a standard seat assignment and baggage. The Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) highlights in-policy fares for easy selection.

**If you're an EMEA or APAC traveler:** Fares that include a seat or baggage may sometimes exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits. If that happens:
- Purchase the seat or baggage **separately** as an ancillary item directly with the airline
- Expense the additional cost separately
- **Save all receipts** for any ancillary purchases

**Important note:** Upgrade fees and **premium seating fees are not reimbursable** — only standard seat selection is covered.

For full details, refer to section 4.7 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy: https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c
